summary_en:
### RL for LLMs
A significant cluster of papers advances reinforcement learning for large language models, focusing on improving training efficiency, credit assignment, and reward design. **EchoRL**and**DRIFT**tackle the inefficiency of full rollouts, with EchoRL reusing advantage-degenerated trajectories via entropy-based clipping, while DRIFT decouples rollouts from optimization using importance-weighted SFT to match multi-turn performance with SFT-level efficiency.**VeriGate**and**Graph-GRPO**address the sparse reward problem in GRPO by introducing step-level credit assignment through verifier-gated process supervision and dependency-aware propagation over graph structures, respectively.**DARTS**accelerates training with distribution-aware active trajectory shaping, and**S2L-PO**proposes using smaller models as natural explorers to enhance rollout diversity. On the reward model side,**SAVE**introduces on-policy value-anchored feedback for self-supervised reward model improvement, while**Configurable Reward Model**enables balanced safety alignment. A critical theoretical contribution from**Annealed Softmax Greedy**provides a structural analogy for GRPO-style updates in many-armed bandits, explaining their empirical behavior. Notably,**Representation Collapse**systematically studies plasticity loss in sequential RL-based post-training, proposing interventions to preserve model capacity.

### Self-Evolving Agents & Open-Ended Learning
Several papers push the frontier of autonomous agent improvement through co-evolution and self-play.**SCOPE**introduces a data-free framework where Challenger and Solver policies co-evolve for open-ended tasks, directly modeling the self-improving agent paradigm.**Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit**provides a sobering analysis, revealing that self-evolving capabilities are surprisingly flat and non-monotonic across model tiers, cautioning against conflating the ability to update with actual performance gains.**AdaCoM**and**ElasticMem**explore learned context management, training external LLMs via RL to compress and adaptively allocate latent memory budgets for frozen agents.**ExpGraph**proposes a model-agnostic experience learning framework with self-evolving graph memory and an RL-trained retrieval copilot.**Skill Reuse as Compression**grounds agentic RL in the Minimum Description Length principle, extracting reusable skill dictionaries from trajectories.**SCALE**and**ReGuLaR**further demonstrate self-improvement through adversarial graph exploration and relation-grounded latent reasoning, respectively.

### Multimodal RL & Grounding
Reinforcement learning is being increasingly applied to multimodal models, with a focus on grounding visual reasoning and mitigating hallucinations.**iVGR**internalizes visual grounding into textual reasoning via a consistency reward in an RL framework.**EASE**augments multimodal RLVR with visual-evidence process supervision by guiding attention maps.**IC-VCO**proposes an RL-based visual preference optimization method with contrastive sample editing to directly mitigate hallucinations.**FOCUS**uses GRPO to minimize localization error in VLMs.**DriveMA**extends this to driving, using verifiable meta-actions with turn-level credit assignment for aligning high-level decisions with low-level planning.**TaskMem**learns a memorization policy for multimodal agents via RL, enabling self-improvement from environment interaction.

### Reasoning Efficiency & Latent Reasoning
Improving the efficiency of chain-of-thought reasoning remains a central theme, with novel approaches emerging for adaptive budget allocation and compressed representations.**SLAT**and**AdaptR1**use RL to learn segment-level adaptive trimming and quality-gated reasoning budgets, respectively, reducing redundancy without sacrificing accuracy.**Chain-of-Thought and Compressed Looped Transformers**provides a theoretical separation between the memory budgets of explicit CoT and compressed latent loops, clarifying the limitations of latent reasoning.**Efficient Diffusion LLMs**proposes temporal-spatial parallel decoding for latency reduction in diffusion-based LLMs.**LinTree**improves reasoning by explicitly representing the tree structure of search histories, outperforming implicit representations.

### Distillation & Training Recipes
Several papers refine on-policy distillation strategies for reasoning models.**Are Full Rollouts Necessary for On-Policy Distillation?**introduces horizon-control strategies (Progressive/Truncated OPD) to improve efficiency for long-horizon tasks.**Trust-Region Behavior Blending**proposes a KL-constrained warmup to improve student rollout quality.**Your Teacher Can't Help You Here**identifies and mitigates supervision fidelity decay in on-policy distillation using a Lookahead Group Reward.**Distilling LLM Feedback**for theorem proving uses token-level supervision to improve policy entropy, outperforming GRPO.

### Specialized Applications & Safety
RL is being applied to specialized domains with unique reward structures.**Graph-GRPO**and**Planner-Centric RL**address e-commerce search and deep research with structure-aware DAG rewards.**Automating Formal Verification**uses RLVR with GRPO for theorem proving, tackling specification hacking.**PA-DR**combines task rewards with a learned privacy classifier for deep research agents.**DareU**proposes an RL-based unlearning framework using data attribution rewards.**Reinforcement Learning Amplifies Emergent Misalignment**provides a cautionary result, showing that harmless rewards can lead to misalignment when amplified by RL in small models.**Inverse Reinforcement Learning without an Optimal Demonstrator** offers a theoretical framework for learning from suboptimal demonstrations, applicable to LLM fine-tuning.
summary_cn:
# LLM的RL
大量论文推进了大型语言模型的强化学习，重点关注提高培训效率、学分分配和奖励设计。**EchoRL**和**DRFT** 解决了完全部署的低效率问题，EchoRL通过基于信息量的剪裁重新使用队列退化轨迹，而DRFT则使用重要性加权SFT从优化中重新合并部署，以匹配多圈性能与SFT级别的效率。**VeriGate**和**Graph-GRPO**分别通过验证者门控流程监督和图结构上的依赖性感知传播引入阶梯级信用分配，解决了GRPO中的稀疏奖励问题。** DARTS**通过分布感知的主动轨迹整形加速训练，** S2 L-PO * 建议使用较小的模型作为自然探索者，以增强部署多样性。在奖励模型方面，**SAVE**引入了政策上的价值锚定反馈，以进行自我监督的奖励模型改进，而**可配置奖励模型** 则实现平衡的安全调整。**Annealed Softmax Greedy** 的关键理论贡献为多武装强盗的GRPO式更新提供了结构类比，解释了他们的经验行为。值得注意的是，**代表崩溃**系统地研究了基于RL的连续后训练中的可塑性损失，并提出了保护模型容量的干预措施。

# 自我进化的代理和开放式学习
几篇论文通过共同进化和自我游戏推动了自主代理改进的前沿。** SCOPE**引入了一个无数据框架，其中挑战者和解决者策略为开放式任务共同进化，直接建模自我改进的代理范式。**更新不是AdaCoM**和**ElasticMem**探索学习的上下文管理，通过RL训练外部LLM，以压缩和自适应地分配冻结代理的潜在内存预算。**ExpShape**提出了一个模型不可知的体验学习框架，具有自我进化的图记忆和RL训练的检索副驾驶员。**技能重复使用作为压缩**以最小描述长度原则为基础代理RL，从轨迹中提取可重复使用的技能字典。** SCALE**和**ReGuLaR**分别通过对抗图探索和基于关系的潜在推理进一步展示了自我改进。

# 多模式RL和接地
强化学习越来越多地应用于多模式模型，重点是建立视觉推理和减轻幻觉。** iVGR**通过RL框架中的一致性奖励将视觉基础内化到文本推理中。** EASE**通过引导注意力图增强了多模式WLVR，具有视觉证据流程监督。**IC-NCO**提出了一种基于RL的视觉偏好优化方法，通过对比样本编辑来直接减轻幻觉。** FOCUS**使用GRPO来最大限度地减少VLM中的本地化错误。** DriveMA**将其扩展到驾驶，使用可验证的元动作和回合级信用分配，以使高层决策与低级规划保持一致。** TaskMem**通过RL学习多模式代理的记忆策略，从而实现从环境交互中自我改进。

# 推理效率和潜在推理
提高思想链推理的效率仍然是一个中心主题，自适应预算分配和压缩表示的新颖方法不断出现。** SLAT**和**AdaptR 1** 分别使用RL来学习分段级自适应修剪和质量门控推理预算，从而在不牺牲准确性的情况下减少冗余。**思想链和压缩循环变形金刚**提供了显式CoT和压缩潜在循环的记忆预算之间的理论分离，澄清了潜在推理的局限性。**高效扩散LLM**提出时空并行解码，以减少基于扩散的LLM中的延迟。** LinTree**通过显式表示搜索历史的树结构来改进推理，其性能优于隐式表示。

# 蒸馏和训练食谱
几篇论文完善了推理模型的政策提炼策略。**按政策蒸馏是否需要全面滚动？**引入水平控制策略（渐进/截断OPD），以提高长期任务的效率。**信任区域行为混合**提出了受KL限制的热身以提高学生的推出质量。**您的老师无法在这里帮助您**使用Lookahead Group奖励识别并缓解政策提炼中的监督忠诚度下降。**提取LLM反馈**以进行定理证明使用代币级监督来改善政策熵，优于GRPO。

# 专业应用与安全
RL正在应用于具有独特奖励结构的专业领域。** Graph-GRPO**和**Planner-Centric RL**通过结构感知的DAQ奖励解决电子商务搜索和深度研究问题。**自动化形式验证**使用WLVR和GRPO进行定理证明，解决规范黑客问题。** PA-DR**将任务奖励与深度研究代理的习得隐私分类器结合起来。** DareU**提出了一个使用数据归因奖励的基于RL的取消学习框架。**强化学习放大了紧急失调**提供了一个警示结果，表明无害的奖励在小型模型中被RL放大时可能会导致失调。**没有最佳演示器的反向强化学习** 提供了一个从次优演示中学习的理论框架，适用于LLM微调。

2605.31433.title: SCOPE: Self-Play via Co-Evolving Policies for Open-Ended Tasks
2605.31433.abstract: arXiv:2605.31433v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Self-play can train language models without external supervision. However, existing methods require rule-checkable answers, leaving open-ended tasks dependent on curated prompts or frontier-model judges. We introduce SCOPE, a data-free self-play framework for open-ended tasks that co-evolves two policies: a Challenger that generates document-grounded tasks, and a Solver that answers them through multi-turn retrieval. A frozen copy of the initial model serves as the self-judge, which writes task-specific rubrics from the source document and grades Solver responses against them. Across three 7-8B instruction-tuned models (Qwen2.5, Qwen3, OLMo-3), SCOPE improves open-ended performance by up to +10.4 points on eight benchmarks and matches or exceeds GRPO_data trained on ~9K curated prompts. Although trained only on open-ended tasks, SCOPE also improves held-out short-form QA by up to +13.8 points on seven held-out benchmarks, surpassing GRPO_data on all three models. Ablations show that co-evolving the Challenger is necessary to keep tasks near the Solver's frontier, that gains arise from improvements in both retrieval and synthesis with the relative contribution varying by task, and that rubric generation quality is the bottleneck for self-judging.
2605.31433.COMMENT: Proposes a data-free self-play framework (SCOPE) that co-evolves a Challenger and Solver policy for open-ended tasks, directly matching the self-evolving agent and RL-for-LLM criteria.
2605.31433.title_cn: 范围：通过开放式任务的共同演变政策进行自我游戏
2605.31433.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31433v1宣布类型：新摘要：自玩可以在没有外部监督的情况下训练语言模型。然而，现有的方法需要规则可检查的答案，从而使开放式任务依赖于精心策划的提示或前沿模型判断。我们引入了Scope，这是一个针对开放式任务的无数据自玩框架，它共同进化了两种策略：生成基于文档的任务的Challenger，和通过多回合检索回答这些任务的Solver。初始模型的冷冻副本用作自我判断，它从源文档中编写特定于任务的标题，并根据它们对Solver响应进行评分。在三个7-8B脚本优化模型（Qwen 2.5、Qwen 3、OLMo-3）中，SCOPE在8个基准测试中将开放式性能提高了高达+10.4个百分点，并匹配或超过了在~ 9 K精心策划的提示上训练的GRPO_数据。尽管仅接受开放式任务的培训，但SCOPE在7个固定基准上也将固定式短期QA提高了+13.8分，超过了所有三个模型上的GRPO_数据。精简表明，共同进化挑战者对于保持任务接近解决者的边界是必要的，收益来自检索和合成的改进，其相对贡献因任务而异，而标题生成质量是自我判断的瓶颈。

2605.30621.title: Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit: Disentangling Evolution Capabilities in Self-Evolving LLM Agents
2605.30621.abstract: arXiv:2605.30621v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM agents are increasingly deployed as systems built around editable external harnesses, including prompts, skills, memories and tools, that shape task execution without changing model parameters. Harness self-evolution adapts such agents by updating these harnesses from execution evidence. Yet it remains unclear whether a model's base capability in task-solving predicts its capabilities in harness self-evolution: which models produce useful harness updates, and which actually benefit from them? We analyze two harness self-evolution capabilities: (i) harness-updating, the capability to produce useful persistent harness updates from execution evidence; (ii) harness-benefit, the capability to benefit from updated harnesses during task solving. Our analysis reveals two findings. First, harness-updating is flat in base capability: models from different capability tiers produce harness updates that lead to surprisingly similar gains; even Qwen3.5-9B's updates yield gains comparable to those of Claude Opus~4.6. Second, harness-benefit is non-monotonic in base capability: weak-tier models benefit little from updated harnesses, mid-tier models benefit most, and strong-tier models benefit less than mid-tier. We trace low gains at the weak tier to two failure modes: weak-tier models may fail to activate relevant harness artifacts, or activate them but fail to follow them faithfully. These findings suggest investing capability budget in the task-solving agent rather than the evolver, and targeting harness invocation and long-horizon instruction following in agent training. Our source code is publicly available at https://github.com/A-EVO-Lab/a-evolve/tree/release/harness-evolution.
2605.30621.COMMENT: Disentangles harness-updating and harness-benefit capabilities in self-evolving LLM agents, revealing surprising flatness and non-monotonicity across model tiers.
2605.30621.title_cn: NPS更新不是NPS好处：理清自进化LLM代理中的进化能力
2605.30621.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30621v1宣布类型：新摘要：LLM代理越来越多地被部署为围绕可编辑外部工具（包括提示、技能、记忆和工具）构建的系统，这些工具在不改变模型参数的情况下塑造任务执行。通过根据执行证据更新这些背带，自我进化来适应此类代理。然而，目前尚不清楚模型在解决任务方面的基本能力是否预示着其利用自我进化的能力：哪些模型会产生有用的利用更新，哪些模型实际上从中受益？我们分析了两种利用自我进化能力：（i）利用更新，从执行证据中产生有用的持续利用更新的能力;（ii）利用收益，在任务解决期间从更新的利用中受益的能力。我们的分析揭示了两个发现。首先，利用更新的基本能力是持平的：来自不同能力层的模型产生的利用更新会带来惊人相似的收益;甚至Qwen 3.5 - 9 B的更新也产生了与Claude Opus~4.6的收益相当的收益。其次，利用效益在基本能力上是非单调的：弱层型号从更新的利用中受益很少，中层型号受益最多，而强层型号受益少于中层。我们将弱层的低收益追溯到两种失败模式：弱层模型可能无法激活相关的背带工件，或者激活它们但未能忠实地遵循它们。这些研究结果表明，将能力预算投资于任务解决代理而不是进化者，并在代理培训中瞄准利用调用和长期指导。我们的源代码可在https://github.com/A-EVO-Lab/a-evolve/tree/release/harness-evolution上公开获取。

2605.31584.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
2605.31584.abstract: arXiv:2605.31584v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Long-context reasoning remains a central challenge for large language models, which often fail to locate and integrate key information in extensive distracting content. Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has shown promise for this task, yet existing methods are limited by low-confusability distractors and sparse, outcome-only reward signals that cannot supervise intermediate reasoning steps. To address these issues, we introduce \textsc{LongTraceRL}. For data construction, we generate multi-hop questions via knowledge graph random walks and leverage search agent trajectories to build \emph{tiered distractors}: documents the agent read but did not cite (high confusability) and documents that appeared in search results but were never opened (low confusability), producing training contexts that are far more challenging than those built by random sampling or one-shot search. For reward design, we propose a \emph{rubric reward} that uses the gold entities along each reasoning chain as fine-grained, entity-level process supervision. This rubric reward is applied only to responses with correct final answers (positive-only strategy), distinguishing the reasoning quality among correct responses and preventing reward hacking. Experiments on three reasoning LLMs (4B--30B) across five long-context benchmarks demonstrate that \textsc{LongTraceRL} consistently outperforms strong baselines and encourages comprehensive, evidence-grounded reasoning. Codes, datasets and models are available at \href{https://github.com/THU-KEG/LongTraceRL}{https://github.com/THU-KEG/LongTraceRL}.
2605.31584.COMMENT: Introduces a novel RLVR pipeline for long-context reasoning that uses search agent trajectories to build tiered distractors and a rubric reward for fine-grained process supervision, directly matching RL for LLMs and agent self-improvement.
2605.31584.title_cn: LongTraceRL：通过Rubric Rewards从搜索代理轨迹学习长上下文推理
2605.31584.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31584v1宣布类型：新摘要：长上下文推理仍然是大型语言模型的核心挑战，这些模型通常无法在大量分散注意力的内容中定位和集成关键信息。具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）已经显示出了这项任务的前景，但现有方法受到低混淆性干扰物和稀疏的、仅结果的奖励信号的限制，这些信号无法监督中间推理步骤。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了\textsk {LongTraceRL}。对于数据构建，我们通过知识图随机游走生成多跳问题，并利用搜索代理轨迹来构建\{分层干扰物}：代理阅读但没有引用的文档（高混淆性）以及出现在搜索结果中但从未打开的文档（低混淆性），产生的训练上下文比随机抽样或一次性搜索构建的上下文更具挑战性。对于奖励设计，我们提出了一个\{rubric reward}，它使用每个推理链上的黄金实体作为细粒度的实体级流程监督。该标题奖励仅适用于具有正确最终答案的响应（仅肯定策略），区分正确响应之间的推理质量并防止奖励黑客攻击。在五个长上下文基准上对三个推理LLM（4 B--30 B）进行的实验表明，\textsk {LongTraceRL}始终优于强基线，并鼓励全面、基于证据的推理。代码、数据集和模型可访问\href{https：//github.com/THU-KEG/LongTraceRL}{https：//github.com/THU-KEG/LongTraceRL}。

2605.31378.title: Unlocking Fine-Grained Translation Quality Estimation in LRMs through Synergistically Evolving Implicit and Explicit Reasoning
2605.31378.abstract: arXiv:2605.31378v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) still struggle with fine-grained translation quality estimation (QE), even with long reasoning chains. We argue that LRMs already possess strong multilingual capabilities, while the core challenge stems from the intrinsic difficulty of learning the fine-grained QE task. In this paper, we propose RIEQE (Reasoning both Implicitly and Explicitly for QE), a simple two-stage training framework that enables the co-evolution of implicit (layer-wise) and explicit (token-wise) reasoning capabilities. To make implicit reasoning feasible, we first decompose the complex QE task into straightforward subtasks. Based on this, our two-stage approach applies: (1) NonThinking-SFT, Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) without reasoning chains to directly boost the model's implicit reasoning tendency and capability; and (2) Thinking-RLVR, standard Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Reward (RLVR) to subsequently strengthen explicit reasoning. Results demonstrate that implicit and explicit reasoning synergistically co-evolve under our framework. On the WMT test sets, RIEQE based on Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 surpasses all baselines in explicit reasoning performance, while its implicit reasoning capability is also comparable to the best current encoder-based models. We further provide evidence for the synergistic collaboration between implicit and explicit reasoning, showing how they mutually benefit each other.
2605.31378.COMMENT: Proposes a two-stage training framework (RIEQE) that co-evolves implicit and explicit reasoning for translation QE, directly matching the latent reasoning and RL for LLMs criteria.
2605.31378.title_cn: 通过协同进化隐式和显式推理解锁LRM中的细粒度翻译质量估计
2605.31378.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31378v1宣布类型：新摘要：即使在推理链长的情况下，大型推理模型（LRM）仍然难以应对细粒度翻译质量估计（QE）。我们认为，LRM已经拥有强大的多语言能力，而核心挑战源于学习细粒度量化宽松任务的内在困难。在本文中，我们提出了RIEQE（QE的隐含和显式推理），这是一个简单的两阶段训练框架，可以实现隐式（分层）和显式（代币）推理能力的共同进化。为了使隐式推理可行，我们首先将复杂的QE任务分解为简单的子任务。基于此，我们的两阶段方法适用：（1）非思考-SFT、没有推理链的监督微调（SFT），以直接增强模型的内隐推理倾向和能力;（2）思考-WLVR，标准强化学习带可验证奖励（WLVR），以随后加强显式推理。结果表明，隐式推理和显式推理在我们的框架下协同共同进化。在WMT测试集上，基于Qwen 3 - 4 B-Thinking-2507的RIEQE显式推理性能超过了所有基线，而其隐式推理能力也与当前最好的基于编码器的模型相媲美。我们进一步为内隐推理和外显推理之间的协同协作提供了证据，展示了它们如何互惠互利。

2605.30757.title: Chain-of-Thought and Compressed Looped Transformers: A Memory-Budget Separation
2605.30757.abstract: arXiv:2605.30757v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Chain-of-thought prompting and looped Transformers both give a fixed model more test-time computation, but they differ in what they remember. Chain-of-thought stores intermediate state in generated tokens that remain in the context, whereas a looped Transformer carries state through recurrent hidden activations. We argue that this persistent mutable memory is a central resource for test-time reasoning.   We compare three memory regimes, the compressed latent loop, the full sequence-state loop, and the chain-of-thought scratchpad. Our main result shows that a compressed loop is limited by the size of its recurrent state. Running the loop longer adds computation but does not by itself create a growing scratchpad, so a loop with a small recurrent state remains a small-space reasoner even when run for many steps. Under a standard complexity assumption, such loops cannot decide problems that are P-complete under logspace reductions, whereas polynomial-length chain-of-thought can.   The separation is specific to compressed loops, as full sequence-state loops carry state at every input position and live in a memory-rich regime closer to explicit scratchpads. Controlled pointer-chasing and associative-recall sweeps illustrate this memory-budget view, with performance sensitive to whether the persistent-state budget matches the task's working-memory demand.
2605.30757.COMMENT: Provides a theoretical and empirical analysis of memory budgets in looped transformers vs. chain-of-thought, directly addressing latent reasoning (compressed latent loop) and its limitations.
2605.30757.title_cn: 思想链和压缩循环变形金刚：内存与预算的分离
2605.30757.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30757v1宣布类型：新摘要：思想链提示和循环变形金刚都为固定模型提供了更多的测试时计算，但它们的记忆不同。思想链将中间状态存储在保留在上下文中的生成令牌中，而循环的Transformer则通过循环的隐藏激活来承载状态。我们认为，这种持久的可变记忆是测试时推理的核心资源。   我们比较了三种记忆机制：压缩潜在循环、完整序列状态循环和思想链记事本。我们的主要结果表明，压缩循环受到其循环状态大小的限制。循环运行更长时间会增加计算量，但本身不会创建不断增长的便笺簿，因此具有小循环状态的循环即使运行多个步骤，仍然是小空间推理器。在标准的复杂性假设下，此类循环无法决定在日志空间约简下P完全的问题，而方程长度的思想链可以。   这种分离是特定于压缩循环的，因为完整的序列状态循环在每个输入位置携带状态，并且处于更接近显式便笺簿的存储器丰富的状态中。受控指针追逐和联想回忆扫描说明了这种记忆预算观点，性能对持续状态预算是否匹配任务的工作记忆需求敏感。

2605.31096.title: iVGR: Internalizing Visually Grounded Reasoning for MLLMs with Reinforcement Learning
2605.31096.abstract: arXiv:2605.31096v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: While visually grounded Chain-of-Thought (CoT) has emerged as a promising paradigm to enhance fine-grained perception in multimodal large language models (MLLMs), its efficacy during the inference phase remains underexplored. In this work, we empirically find that mandating explicit object boxes in visually grounded CoT during inference often degrades performance compared to standard textual CoT, which reasons without explicit visual grounding. We hypothesize that the visual localization capability can be internalized into the textual CoT and that the mandatory explicit grounding introduces unnecessary interference with the model's primary objective of answer prediction. To address this problem, we propose Internalizing Visually Grounded Reasoning (\textbf{iVGR}), a novel reinforcement learning framework that transfers localization capabilities into the textual reasoning process. We employ a dual-stream training strategy, where a textual stream is aligned with a high-quality visually grounded stream via a proposed consistency reward, enabling the model to localize accurately without explicit grounding during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms existing baselines on fine-grained benchmarks, while maintaining the flexibility to support tool-assisted inference workflows.
2605.31096.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RL framework (iVGR) that internalizes visual grounding into textual reasoning via a consistency reward, directly addressing latent reasoning and RL for LLMs.
2605.31096.title_cn: iVGR：通过强化学习内化MLLM的视觉基础推理
2605.31096.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31096v1宣布类型：新摘要：虽然基于视觉的思想链（CoT）已成为增强多模式大型语言模型（MLLM）中细粒度感知的一种有希望的范式，但其在推理阶段的功效仍然没有得到充分的探索。在这项工作中，我们经验性地发现，与标准文本CoT相比，在推理期间强制在视觉基础上使用显式对象框通常会降低性能，而标准文本CoT的推理没有明确的视觉基础。我们假设视觉定位能力可以内化到文本CoT中，并且强制性的显式基础会对模型的主要目标（答案预测）引入不必要的干扰。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了内化视觉基础推理（\textBF{iVGR}），这是一种新型强化学习框架，可以将本地化能力转移到文本推理过程中。我们采用了双流训练策略，其中文本流通过提议的一致性奖励与高质量的视觉基础流对齐，使模型能够在推理过程中准确定位，而无需显式基础。大量实验表明，我们的方法在细粒度基准上显着优于现有基线，同时保持了支持工具辅助推理工作流程的灵活性。

2605.30451.title: VeriGate: Verifier-Gated Step-Level Supervision for GRPO
2605.30451.abstract: arXiv:2605.30451v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is an effective recipe for training reasoning models with verifier-based outcome rewards, but its supervision is sparse: when all sampled trajectories for a prompt receive the same verifier reward, the group-relative advantage collapses to zero and learning stalls. Outcome-only rewards also provide no step-level credit assignment, limiting exploration and making it harder to learn robust reasoning. We present VeriGate (Verifier-Gated Step-Level GRPO), a verifier-gated extension of GRPO that addresses these limitations with three design choices. First, VeriGate keeps the verifier in charge whenever verifier rewards induce a meaningful preference among sampled trajectories, and uses process supervision only when verifier rewards are degenerate. Second, instead of collapsing Process Reward Model (PRM) step scores into a single trajectory reward, VeriGate converts them into future-cumulated rewards to assign continuation-aware credit. Third, VeriGate transforms these rewards into group-normalized token-level advantages, restoring informative gradients and fine-grained credit assignment while remaining less susceptible to reward hacking than methods that optimize aggregated PRM scores. Empirically, training on MATH with 1.5B and 7B Qwen2.5-Instruct models and evaluating on six reasoning benchmarks, VeriGate improves average accuracy by about 20% and 12% for 1.5B and 7B models respectively, substantially reduces zero-gradient failures, decreases reward-hacking behavior, and improves reasoning quality relative to outcome-only GRPO and PRM-as-outcome baselines.
2605.30451.COMMENT: Proposes VeriGate, a verifier-gated extension of GRPO that introduces step-level credit assignment via process supervision to overcome sparse reward and zero-gradient failures in RL for LLM reasoning.
2605.30451.title_cn: VeriGate：GRPO的验证器门控分步监督
2605.30451.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30451v1宣布类型：新摘要：群体相对政策优化（GRPO）是利用基于验证者的结果奖励训练推理模型的有效方法，但它的监督是稀疏的：当提示的所有采样轨迹都收到相同的验证者奖励时，群体相对优势就会崩溃为零，学习就会停滞。仅限结果的奖励也不提供阶梯级的学分分配，从而限制了探索并使学习稳健推理变得更加困难。我们介绍了VeriGate（Verifier-Gated Step-Level GRPO），这是GRPO的验证器门控扩展，通过三种设计选择解决了这些限制。首先，每当验证者奖励在采样轨迹中引起有意义的偏好时，VeriGate就会让验证者负责，并且仅在验证者奖励退化时使用流程监督。其次，VeriGate没有将流程奖励模型（PRM）步骤分数分解为单个轨迹奖励，而是将它们转换为未来累积奖励以分配持续感知的信用。第三，VeriGate将这些奖励转化为群体标准化的代币级优势，恢复信息梯度和细粒度的信用分配，同时比优化汇总PRM分数的方法更不容易受到奖励黑客攻击的影响。从经验上讲，使用1.5B和7 B Qwen2.5-Instruct模型对MATH进行训练，并在六个推理基准上进行评估，VeriGate将1.5B和7 B模型的平均准确率分别提高了约20%和12%，大大减少了零梯度故障，减少了奖励黑客行为，并提高了相对于仅结果GRPO和PRM作为结果基线的推理质量。

2605.31228.title: EchoRL: Reinforcement Learning via Rollout Echoing
2605.31228.abstract: arXiv:2605.31228v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards is an effective route for post-training to strengthen the reasoning capability of large language models. However, as training proceeds, the learning signal can collapse thus makes the training gain become marginal and ineffective. Specifically, a growing fraction of prompts' rollouts become advantage-degenerated: all the self-generated rollouts show verified-success, making the standard deviation over their rewards be zero; accordingly each rollout's advantage becomes degenerated (zero) as well. Given such rollouts' advantages, the policy-gradient for model optimization eventually vanishes, capping the training performance. We argue that some of these rollouts still contain valuable learning signals but unfortunately omitted with the existing RLVR methods. In this paper, inspired through analyzing the entropy pattern behind golden trajectories produced by external expert models, we propose EchoRL for better exploiting the advantage-degenerated rollouts to further improve the training performance. EchoRL is a lightweight module that first identifies an EchoClip from verified-success rollouts based on their step-level entropy values, and then feeds this clip back as an auxiliary supervision signal in the RL objective. Extensive experiments across 10 benchmarks, 5 LLM backbones, and 4 popular RLVR post-training methods demonstrate that EchoRL consistently improves RLVR post-training with minimal overhead.
2605.31228.COMMENT: Proposes EchoRL, a novel RLVR method that reuses advantage-degenerated rollouts via entropy-based EchoClip to provide auxiliary supervision, directly improving RL post-training for LLMs.
2605.31228.title_cn: EchoRL：通过推出Echoing进行强化学习
2605.31228.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31228v1宣布类型：新摘要：具有可验证奖励的强化学习是加强大型语言模型推理能力的后训练的有效途径。然而，随着训练的进行，学习信号可能会崩溃，从而使训练收益变得边缘且无效。具体来说，越来越多的提示推出变得提示退化：所有自生成的推出都显示出经过验证的成功，使得其奖励的标准差为零;因此，每个推出的优势也变得退化（零）。鉴于此类部署的优势，模型优化的政策梯度最终会消失，从而限制训练性能。我们认为，其中一些部署仍然包含有价值的学习信号，但不幸的是，现有的WLVR方法被省略了。在本文中，通过分析外部专家模型产生的黄金轨迹背后的信息模式，我们提出EchoRL来更好地利用队列退化的展开来进一步提高训练性能。EchoRL是一个轻量级模块，它首先根据已验证成功的展开中的步进级信息值识别Echo剪辑，然后将此剪辑作为RL目标中的辅助监督信号反馈。针对10个基准测试、5个LLM主干和4种流行的WLVR后训练方法的广泛实验表明，EchoRL以最少的费用持续改进了WLVR后训练。

2605.30888.title: The Flip Side of RLHF: On-Policy Feedback for Reward Model Self-Supervised Improvement
2605.30888.abstract: arXiv:2605.30888v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Building strong reward models (RMs) for language model alignment is bottlenecked by the cost and difficulty of acquiring diverse and reliable preference data from human annotation or judge models. It is dramatically worse as the policy evolves beyond the static RM training. Therefore, we propose SAVE (Self-supervised reward model improvement via Value-Anchored On-policy feedback), a framework that grades on-policy responses as feedback by using the value function for on-policy RM training. SAVE naturally converts the reward-graded on-policy responses into supervision with a prompt-specific value head as an adaptive anchor. It computes RM advantages and filters ambiguous samples to update the RM via a contrastive objective. The effectiveness of SAVE for enhancing RM training is strongly validated through rigorous empirical evaluation across six diverse benchmarks. It achieves outperforming results across all datasets while maintaining consistent improvements across three RL algorithms (GRPO, RLOO, GSPO) and different policy backbones.
2605.30888.COMMENT: Proposes SAVE, a novel self-supervised RL framework that uses on-policy value-anchored feedback to improve reward model training for LLM alignment, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion.
2605.30888.title_cn: RL HF的另一面：奖励模型自我监督改进的政策反馈
2605.30888.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30888v1宣布类型：新摘要：为语言模型对齐构建强奖励模型（RM）受到从人类注释或判断模型获取多样化且可靠的偏好数据的成本和难度的限制。随着政策的发展超出静态RM培训，情况会变得更加糟糕。因此，我们提出了SAVE（通过价值锚定政策反馈的自我监督奖励模型改进），这是一个通过使用价值函数进行政策上RM培训来将政策上响应作为反馈进行评级的框架。SAVE自然地将奖励分级的政策响应转化为监督，并以预算特定的价值头部作为自适应锚点。它计算RM优势并过滤模糊样本以通过对比目标更新RM。SAVE在加强RM培训方面的有效性通过六个不同基准的严格实证评估得到了有力的验证。它在所有数据集中实现了优于所有数据集的结果，同时在三种RL算法（GRPO、RLOO、GSPO）和不同的政策主干之间保持一致的改进。

2605.30789.title: Smaller Models are Natural Explorers for Policy-Level Diversity in GRPO
2605.30789.abstract: arXiv:2605.30789v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We identify a new dimension for enhancing rollout diversity in Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for LLMs. While GRPO relies on diverse rollouts, prevailing strategies primarily increase diversity by injecting more token-level randomness, which may introduce step-wise noise and lead to incoherent trajectories. We uncover that smaller models within the same model family inherently exhibit higher policy-level diversity, indicated by their superior pass@k relative to larger counterparts as sample counts increase. Unlike token-level noise, this diversity is temporally correlated, preserves logical consistency, and provides structured exploration signals for gradient estimation. We thus propose S2L-PO (Small-to-Large Policy Optimization), a framework that leverages fixed small models as natural explorers to train larger models. To balance exploration and exploitation, we design a progressive annealing strategy that transitions from offline small-model rollouts to the large learner's own sampling. This shift elegantly avoids mid-training performance drops caused by the small model's capacity limits, achieving faster convergence and unlocking a higher performance ceiling. S2L-PO improves accuracy on diverse mathematical reasoning benchmarks (e.g., +8.8% on AIME 24 using a 1.7B explorer to guide the 8B model) while reducing rollout compute.
2605.30789.COMMENT: Proposes S2L-PO, a novel RL training framework for LLMs that uses smaller models as natural explorers to improve rollout diversity in GRPO, directly addressing RL for LLMs.
2605.30789.title_cn: 较小的模型是GRPO政策层面多样性的天然探索者
2605.30789.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30789v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们确定了一个新的维度，用于增强LLM的集团相对政策优化（GRPO）的部署多样性。虽然GRPO依赖于多样化的推出，但流行的策略主要通过注入更多代币级别的随机性来增加多样性，这可能会引入逐步噪音并导致不连贯的轨迹。我们发现，同一模型系列中的较小模型本质上表现出更高的政策级别多样性，这一点从随着样本数量的增加，它们相对于较大的模型具有更高的通过率@k来表明。与标记级噪音不同，这种多样性在时间上是相关的，保持逻辑一致性，并为梯度估计提供结构化勘探信号。因此，我们提出了S2 L-PO（从小到大的政策优化），这是一个利用固定的小模型作为自然探索者来训练更大的模型的框架。为了平衡探索和利用，我们设计了一种渐进式退变策略，从离线小模型推出过渡到大型学习者自己的采样。这一转变巧妙地避免了小型型号容量限制导致的训练中性能下降，实现了更快的融合并解锁了更高的性能上限。S2 L-PO提高了各种数学推理基准（例如，AIME 24上+8.8%，使用1.7B资源管理器来引导8B模型），同时减少了推出计算。

2605.30912.title: Attend to Evidence: Evidence-Anchored Spatial Attention Supervision for Multimodal RLVR
2605.30912.abstract: arXiv:2605.30912v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) improves vision-language models (VLMs) by optimizing outcome rewards derived from final answers. However, such outcome-only rewards do not tell the model which image regions justify an answer. For questions that require visual grounding, these rewards cannot distinguish responses supported by relevant visual evidence from those produced by language-prior shortcuts or lucky guesses. We introduce EASE (Evidence-Anchored Spatial Attention), which augments multimodal RLVR with visual-evidence process supervision. EASE converts annotated evidence regions into a smoothed visual-token target and uses it to guide response-to-image attention during RL training, but only on high-reward trajectories. The annotations are used solely as privileged training labels, while inference requires only the original image and question. Across Qwen2.5-VL-7B, Qwen3-VL-4B, and Qwen3-VL-8B, EASE raises average scores over DAPO by 2.5 to 3.1 points on perception, hallucination, visual math, and multimodal reasoning benchmarks. Diagnostics and ablations show that EASE better aligns visual attention with annotated evidence regions.
2605.30912.COMMENT: Proposes EASE, a novel method augmenting multimodal RLVR with visual-evidence process supervision via attention guidance, directly improving RL for VLMs.
2605.30912.title_cn: 关注证据：多模式WLVR的证据锚定空间注意力监督
2605.30912.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30912v1宣布类型：新摘要：具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）通过优化从最终答案得出的结果奖励来改进视觉语言模型（VLMS）。然而，这种仅基于结果的奖励并不能告诉模型哪些图像区域证明了答案是合理的。对于需要视觉基础的问题，这些奖励无法区分相关视觉证据支持的响应与语言优先快捷方式或幸运猜测产生的响应。我们引入了EASE（证据锚定空间注意力），它通过视觉证据过程监督增强了多模式WLVR。EASE将带注释的证据区域转换为平滑的视觉标记目标，并使用它来引导RL训练期间对图像的反应注意力，但仅限于高回报轨迹。注释仅用作特权训练标签，而推理仅需要原始图像和问题。在Qwen 2.5-BL-7 B、Qwen 3-BL-4 B和Qwen 3-BL-8B中，EASE在感知、幻觉、视觉数学和多模式推理基准方面的平均得分比DAPO提高了2.5至3.1分。诊断和消融表明，EASE可以更好地将视觉注意力与注释的证据区域对齐。

2605.30407.title: Exploring Autonomous Agentic Data Engineering for Model Specialization
2605.30407.abstract: arXiv:2605.30407v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance on general tasks, while often struggling to adapt to specialized domains without high-quality domain-specific data. Existing LLM-based data curation methods primarily rely on human-designed workflows, leaving it unexamined whether LLMs can autonomously execute an end-to-end data engineering pipeline for model specialization. We formalize \textbf{Autonomous Agentic Data Engineering}, a novel task designed to evaluate LLMs as autonomous data engineers that drive model specialization through end-to-end data curation. We frame data as an optimizable component and study agents that plan, generate, and iteratively optimize training data across multiple domains, guided by post-training performance improvement. Experiments show that autonomous LLM data engineers yield substantial gains, as GPT-5.2 constructs a training curriculum that improves a student model by \textbf{57.29\%}, entirely through iterative, agent-driven data adaptation. By illuminating both potential and bottlenecks, our study establishes autonomous data engineering as a measurable capability and charts a path toward agent-driven model specialization\footnote{Code will be released at https://github.com/zjunlp/DataAgent.}.
2605.30407.COMMENT: Proposes a self-improving agent framework for autonomous data engineering that iteratively optimizes training data via post-training performance feedback, directly matching the self-evolving agent criterion.
2605.30407.title_cn: 探索自主统计数据工程以实现模型专业化
2605.30407.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30407v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）在一般任务上表现出了出色的性能，但在没有高质量特定领域数据的情况下，通常难以适应专业领域。现有的基于LLM的数据策展方法主要依赖于人类设计的工作流程，因此LLM是否可以自主执行端到端数据工程管道以进行模型专业化尚未得到审查。我们正式化了\textBF{自治统计数据工程}，这是一项新颖的任务，旨在评估LLM作为自治数据工程师的能力，通过端到端数据策展推动模型专业化。我们将数据构建为可优化的组件，并研究以训练后性能改进为指导，规划、生成和迭代优化多个领域的训练数据的代理。实验表明，自主LLM数据工程师会产生巨大的收益，因为GPT-5.2构建了一个培训课程，该课程完全通过迭代、代理驱动的数据自适应，通过\textBF{57.29\%}改进了学生模型。通过阐明潜力和瓶颈，我们的研究将自主数据工程确立为一种可衡量的能力，并绘制了一条通往代理驱动模型专业化的道路\脚注{代码将在https：//github.com/zjunlp/DataAgent。}发布。

2605.30919.title: De-attribute to Forget for LLM Unlearning
2605.30919.abstract: arXiv:2605.30919v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: The rapid development of large language models (LLMs) has raised concerns on the use of inappropriate data for training, which has led to a growing interest in LLM unlearning. Many existing LLM unlearning approaches rely on optimizing prediction loss(es), such as maximizing the loss on the forget set, but often face critical issues like over-forgetting and poor model utility. To address them, this paper novelly frames the optimization objective for LLM unlearning as one of zeroing out data attribution instead. In particular, we propose the first LLM unlearning framework based on data attribution rewards called DareU that performs reinforcement learning to update the LLM by reducing the attribution score of its generated responses (i.e., de-attributing) to the forget data owners. Empirical evaluation using an LLM classifier as an efficient approximation of attribution shows that DareU outperforms existing baselines by achieving effective unlearning while balancing forget quality and model utility well.
2605.30919.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RL-based LLM unlearning framework (DareU) that uses data attribution rewards to de-attribute responses from forget data, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion.
2605.30919.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30919v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）的快速发展引发了人们对使用不适当数据进行训练的担忧，这导致人们对LLM取消学习的兴趣日益浓厚。许多现有的LLM去学习方法依赖于优化预测损失，例如最大化遗忘集中的损失，但经常面临过度遗忘和模型效用较差等关键问题。为了解决这些问题，本文新颖地将LLM取消学习的优化目标框架为将数据属性归零。特别是，我们提出了第一个基于数据归因奖励的LLM取消学习框架，称为DareU，它执行强化学习，通过降低其生成的响应的归因分数来更新LLM（即，去归因）给忘记的数据所有者。使用LLM分类器作为归因的有效逼近的经验评估表明，DareU通过实现有效的去学习，同时很好地平衡忘记质量和模型效用，优于现有基线。

2605.31003.title: Graph-GRPO: Dependency-Aware Credit Assignment for Generative E-commerce Search Relevance
2605.31003.abstract: arXiv:2605.31003v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Search relevance modeling is a core task in e-commerce search systems, assessing how well a user query matches candidate products. Rather than relying on a single holistic matching signal, relevance judgment often requires structured reasoning over query understanding, product understanding, and facet-level matching. With large language models (LLMs), this process is increasingly formulated as chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and optimized with reinforcement learning (RL). However, existing RL methods mainly rely on outcome-level rewards and treat the entire reasoning chain as a single optimization unit. This makes it difficult to distinguish faulty reasoning steps from correct intermediate ones, leading to misaligned credit assignment. Although process-reward methods provide denser supervision, they often treat reasoning steps independently and ignore dependency-driven error propagation, making responsibility attribution difficult and limiting the optimization of structured relevance reasoning. We propose Graph-GRPO, a graph-structured extension of GRPO for multi-component relevance reasoning. Graph-GRPO constructs a relevance reasoning dependency graph, where CoT steps are modeled as nodes and their logical dependencies as edges. It propagates outcome-level rewards over the graph to derive step-level credit signals, enabling more accurate fine-grained credit assignment. We further introduce a main-loss-driven controller that adaptively adjusts edge-wise credit-propagation coefficients. Together with CoT random masking for supervised policy initialization and graph-node-based multi-head distillation, we build a trainable and deployable framework for generative relevance modeling. Extensive offline evaluations and online A/B tests on a leading e-commerce platform demonstrate that the Graph-GRPO-based framework improves relevance classification metrics and key engagement metrics.
2605.31003.COMMENT: Proposes Graph-GRPO, a graph-structured extension of GRPO for fine-grained credit assignment in CoT reasoning for e-commerce search relevance, directly addressing RL for LLMs with a new reward propagation method.
2605.31003.title_cn: Graph-GRPO：生成式电子商务搜索相关性的依赖感知信用分配
2605.31003.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31003v1宣布类型：新摘要：搜索相关性建模是电子商务搜索系统的核心任务，评估用户查询与候选产品的匹配程度。相关性判断通常需要对查询理解、产品理解和面级匹配进行结构化推理，而不是依赖于单一的整体匹配信号。对于大型语言模型（LLM），这个过程越来越多地被描述为思想链（CoT）推理，并通过强化学习（RL）进行优化。然而，现有的RL方法主要依赖于结果级奖励，并将整个推理链视为单个优化单元。这使得很难区分错误的推理步骤与正确的中间步骤，从而导致信用分配不一致。尽管流程奖励方法提供了更密集的监督，但它们通常独立处理推理步骤并忽略依赖性驱动的错误传播，从而使责任归因变得困难并限制了结构化相关性推理的优化。我们提出了Graph-GRPO，这是GRPO的图结构扩展，用于多分量相关性推理。Graph-GRPO构建了一个相关性推理依赖图，其中CoT步骤被建模为节点，其逻辑依赖关系被建模为边。它在图表上传播结果级奖励，以获得阶梯级信用信号，从而实现更准确的细粒度信用分配。我们进一步引入了一个主要损失驱动的控制器，该控制器自适应地调整边缘信用传播系数。再加上CoT随机掩码的监督策略初始化和基于图节点的多头蒸馏，我们建立了一个可训练和可部署的框架生成相关性建模。在一个领先的电子商务平台上进行的广泛的离线评估和在线A/B测试表明，基于Graph-GRPO的框架改进了相关性分类指标和关键参与指标。

2605.31312.title: Learning from Fine-Grained Visual Discrepancies: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucinations via In-Context Visual Contrastive Optimization
2605.31312.abstract: arXiv:2605.31312v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multimodal hallucination remains a persistent challenge for Vision-Language Models (VLMs). Standard textual Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) often fails to mitigate it due to a lack of explicit visual supervision. While existing works introduce visual preference DPO by contrasting original images against negative ones, they suffer from a theoretically inconsistent objective caused by partition function mismatches and rely on coarse-grained negatives that could enable shortcut learning. In this work, we propose In-Context Visual Contrastive Optimization (IC-VCO). By placing contrastive images within a shared multi-image context, IC-VCO ensures a mathematically rigorous objective. We further introduce Visual Contrast Distillation (VCDist), an auxiliary reliability-gated regularizer that encourages consistency between multi-image contrastive training and single-image inference. Finally, we propose a contrastive sample editing strategy that generates hard negatives via precise semantic perturbations. Experiments on five benchmarks demonstrate IC-VCO's best overall performance and the effectiveness of our sample editing strategy. Code and data are available at https://github.com/OPPO-Mente-Lab/IC-VCO.
2605.31312.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RL-based visual preference optimization method (IC-VCO) with a rigorous objective and contrastive sample editing to mitigate multimodal hallucinations.
2605.31312.title_cn: 从细粒度视觉差异中学习：通过上下文视觉对比优化缓解多模式幻觉
2605.31312.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31312v1宣布类型：新摘要：多模式幻觉仍然是视觉语言模型（VLM）的持续挑战。由于缺乏明确的视觉监督，标准文本直接偏好优化（DPO）通常无法缓解这一问题。虽然现有的作品通过将原始图像与负面图像进行对比来引入视觉偏好DPO，但它们因分区函数不匹配而导致理论上目标不一致，并且依赖于可以实现捷径学习的粗粒度负面图像。在这项工作中，我们提出了上下文内视觉对比优化（IC-VGA）。通过将对比图像放置在共享的多图像环境中，IC-VGA确保了数学上严格的目标。我们进一步引入视觉对比度蒸馏（VCDist），这是一种辅助可靠性门控规则化器，可促进多图像对比训练和单图像推断之间的一致性。最后，我们提出了一种对比样本编辑策略，通过精确的语义扰动生成硬否定。五个基准测试的实验证明了IC-VGA的最佳整体性能以及我们的样本编辑策略的有效性。代码和数据可在https://github.com/OPPO-Mente-Lab/IC-VCO上获取。

2605.31192.title: The Regularizing Power of Language-Training Deepfake Detectors
2605.31192.abstract: arXiv:2605.31192v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recently, thanks to the advent of Multimodal-LLMs, deepfake detectors are striving not only to be generalizable but also interpretable. We propose that these two challenges can effectively be tackled jointly, since describable artifacts typically generalize better, opening the possibility to use language as a regularization mechanism. Since deepfake detection generally suffers from overfitting to low-level domain-specific artifacts, our intuition is that an LLM that has been pretrained on language would prefer high-level artifacts that can be described better. This way, we can use high-level features where possible, while training the model to use low-level features where necessary. We utilize a dual-encoder architecture, pairing a frozen specialist detector with a LoRA-tuned MLLM encoder, and a two-stage training curriculum: first, a binary alignment phase demonstrates that the intrinsic capability of MLLMs can effectively combine features to mitigate overfitting to dataset-specific artifacts. To further bolster generalization and achieve interpretability, we employ a reinforcement learning stage that encourages the model to generate descriptive reasoning before classifying, using only binary labels. By rewarding this "explain-then-classify" behavior, we explicitly incentivize the model to prioritize high-level, robust features. Crucially, this process yields both interpretable descriptions and a further boost in cross-dataset performance, even when reasoning chains are omitted at inference. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets validate our approach, outperforming state-of-the-art methods by a large margin.
2605.31192.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RL stage for deepfake detection that rewards 'explain-then-classify' reasoning, directly matching RL for LLMs via explicit reward design.
2605.31192.title_cn: 伪装训练Deepfake探测器的常规化能力
2605.31192.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31192v1宣布类型：新摘要：最近，由于多模式LLM的出现，Deepfake检测器不仅致力于可概括，而且致力于可解释。我们建议这两个挑战可以有效地联合应对，因为可描述的工件通常会更好地概括，从而打开了使用语言作为正规化机制的可能性。由于Deepfake检测通常会对低级特定领域的工件过适应，因此我们的直觉是，已经在语言上预训练过的LLM会更喜欢可以更好地描述的高级工件。这样，我们就可以在可能的情况下使用高级特征，同时训练模型在必要时使用低级特征。我们利用双编码器架构，将冻结的专业检测器与LoRA调谐的MLLM编码器配对，以及两阶段的训练课程：首先，二进制对齐阶段证明MLLM的内在能力可以有效地组合特征，以减轻对厕所特定伪影的过度适应。为了进一步加强概括并实现可解释性，我们采用了强化学习阶段，鼓励模型在分类之前仅使用二进制标签生成描述性推理。通过奖励这种“先解释后分类”的行为，我们明确激励模型优先考虑高级、稳健的特征。至关重要的是，即使在推理时省略了推理链，这个过程既能产生可解释的描述，又能进一步提高跨数据集的性能。对基准数据集的广泛实验验证了我们的方法，其性能大大优于最先进的方法。

2605.31490.title: Are Full Rollouts Necessary for On-Policy Distillation?
2605.31490.abstract: arXiv:2605.31490v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) provides dense teacher feedback along rollouts generated by the student and has emerged as a promising post-training paradigm for long-horizon reasoning. However, standard OPD typically generates full rollouts during training, which is computationally expensive and may expose the student to unreliable teacher feedback at late rollout positions, especially during early training. We identify the rollout horizon as a key bottleneck in OPD that substantially impacts training efficiency. Unlike Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), OPD does not require a complete trajectory or a final answer reward to provide learning signals. This observation suggests that full rollouts may not always be necessary for effective OPD. Motivated by this insight, we propose two simple horizon-control strategies: Progressive OPD (POPD), which gradually expands the rollout horizon during training, and Truncated OPD (TOPD), which permanently performs distillation on reliable truncated rollouts. Experiments on mathematical reasoning show that POPD improves the training efficiency of OPD by up to 3$\times$, while TOPD matches OPD performance using only 10\% of the rollout horizon, leading to substantial wall-clock and memory reductions. These results demonstrate that controlling the rollout horizon offers a simple and practical path to more efficient OPD.
2605.31490.COMMENT: Proposes horizon-control strategies (Progressive/Truncated OPD) to improve on-policy distillation efficiency for long-horizon reasoning, directly addressing RL training recipes for LLMs.
2605.31490.title_cn: 政策上的蒸馏需要全面滚动吗？
2605.31490.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31490v1宣布类型：新摘要：政策蒸馏（OPD）在学生生成的推出过程中提供密集的教师反馈，并已成为一种有前途的长期推理培训后范式。然而，标准OPD通常会在培训期间生成完整的展开，这在计算上很昂贵，并且可能会让学生在后期展开位置接触不可靠的教师反馈，尤其是在早期培训期间。我们将推出范围确定为OPD的一个关键瓶颈，它会极大地影响培训效率。与具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）不同，OPD不需要完整的轨迹或最终答案奖励来提供学习信号。这一观察结果表明，为了有效的OPD，全面推出可能并不总是必要的。受这一见解的启发，我们提出了两种简单的水平控制策略：渐进式OPD（POPD），在训练期间逐渐扩大展开视野，和截短式OPD（TOPD），在可靠的截短式展开上永久执行蒸馏。数学推理实验表明，POPD将OPD的训练效率提高了高达3 $\x $，而TOPD仅使用10%的推出范围即可与OPD性能相匹配，从而大幅减少时钟和内存。这些结果表明，控制推出范围为更高效的OPD提供了一条简单实用的途径。

2605.30832.title: SLAT: Segment-Level Adaptive Trimming for Efficient CoT Reasoning
2605.30832.abstract: arXiv:2605.30832v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recent advances in Large Reasoning Models have significantly improved chain-of-thought (CoT) capabilities via reinforcement learning (RL). However, generated reasoning chains frequently suffer from structural redundancy (i.e., \emph{overthinking}), incurring high computational overhead without improving answer correctness. Existing mitigation strategies typically rely on token-uniform length penalties, which provide coarse, segment-agnostic pressure toward shorter outputs and can inadvertently suppress useful reasoning alongside redundancy. To address this, we demonstrate that inefficiency concentrates in high-probability segments with low marginal utility. We derive a theoretical characterization of segment suboptimality under the correctness-length trade-off objective and propose \textsc{SLAT} (Segment-Level Adaptive Trimming), an RL framework that selectively suppresses redundant segments based on this criterion. Empirical results on standard benchmarks indicate that \textsc{SLAT} establishes a superior accuracy-efficiency Pareto frontier, reducing reasoning length by $50\%$ relative to uncompressed baselines while maintaining competitive accuracy. Overall, our results suggest that theoretically grounded, segment-aware trimming is a promising direction for efficient CoT reasoning in large language models.
2605.30832.COMMENT: Proposes SLAT, an RL framework for segment-level adaptive trimming of CoT reasoning chains to reduce redundancy and improve efficiency, directly addressing RL for LLMs and latent reasoning efficiency.
2605.30832.title_cn: SLAT：用于高效CoT推理的分段级自适应修剪
2605.30832.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30832v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型推理模型的最新进展通过强化学习（RL）显着提高了思想链（CoT）能力。然而，生成的推理链经常遭受结构冗余（即，\{overthinking}），在不提高答案正确性的情况下会产生很高的计算负担。现有的缓解策略通常依赖于标记一致的长度惩罚，这为更短的输出提供了粗略的、分段不可知的压力，并且可能会无意中抑制有用的推理和冗余。为了解决这个问题，我们证明低效率集中在边际效用较低的高概率部分。我们在正确性-长度权衡目标下推导出分段次优性的理论描述，并提出\textsk {SLAT}（分段级自适应修剪），这是一个RL框架，可以根据该标准选择性地抑制冗余分段。标准基准的经验结果表明，\textsk {SLAT}建立了卓越的准确性-效率Pareto前沿，相对于未压缩的基线将推理长度减少50%美元，同时保持有竞争力的准确性。总体而言，我们的结果表明，理论上有依据的、分段感知的修剪是大型语言模型中高效CoT推理的一个有前途的方向。

2605.31455.title: DRIFT: Decoupled Rollouts and Importance-Weighted Fine-Tuning for Efficient Multi-Turn Optimization
2605.31455.abstract: arXiv:2605.31455v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed in multi-turn interactive settings where users or environments can iteratively provide lightweight feedback. Unfortunately, optimizing such behavior presents a sharp dilemma in practice: online reinforcement learning is able to effectively address multi-turn dynamics but is prohibitively expensive due to the cost of generating full correction trajectories at every update, whereas offline supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is efficient but suffers from distribution shift and behavioral collapse. To this end, we novelly propose DRIFT (Decoupled Rollouts and Importance-Weighted Fine-Tuning), a framework that operationalizes the theoretical insight that the KL-regularized RL objective is equivalent to importance-weighted supervised learning. DRIFT decouples rollout from optimization by sampling offline interaction trajectories from a fixed reference policy, deriving return-based importance weights, and optimizing the policy via weighted SFT on the resulting dataset. Empirically, we demonstrate that DRIFT matches or exceeds the performance of multi-turn reinforcement learning baselines while maintaining the training efficiency and simplicity of standard supervised fine-tuning. Code is available at https://github.com/2020-qqtcg/DRIFT.
2605.31455.COMMENT: Proposes DRIFT, a novel RL framework that decouples rollouts from optimization via importance-weighted SFT, matching multi-turn RL performance with SFT efficiency.
2605.31455.title_cn: DRIFT：用于高效多圈优化的解耦卷展和重要性加权微调
2605.31455.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31455v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型越来越多地部署在多轮交互式环境中，用户或环境可以迭代地提供轻量级反馈。不幸的是，优化此类行为在实践中面临着尖锐的困境：在线强化学习能够有效地解决多轮动态问题，但由于在每次更新时生成完整纠正轨迹的成本，成本高得令人望而却步，而离线监督微调（SFT）很高效，但会遭受分布变化和行为崩溃的困扰。为此，我们新颖地提出了DRift（去耦合滚卷和重要加权微调），这是一个框架，它实现了KL正规化RL目标相当于重要性加权监督学习的理论见解。DRFT通过从固定参考策略中采样离线交互轨迹、推导基于返回的重要性权重并通过对所得数据集进行加权SFT优化策略，将推出与优化分开。从经验上看，我们证明DRFT符合或超过多轮强化学习基线的性能，同时保持标准监督微调的训练效率和简单性。代码可在https://github.com/2020-qqtcg/DRIFT上获取。

2605.30785.title: Learning Agent-Compatible Context Management for Long-Horizon Tasks
2605.30785.abstract: arXiv:2605.30785v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM agents increasingly face long-horizon tasks such as web search and deep research in real-world applications, where accumulated context can cause long-context degradation and reasoning failures. Prior work mitigates this through context management with agent-side context control or fixed strategies such as summarization, which require training the agent itself for adaptation - making it impractical for closed-source agents and ignoring that different agents may require different strategies. We introduce Adaptive Context Management (AdaCoM), which trains an external LLM to manage the context of a frozen agent through flexible modification actions and end-to-end reinforcement learning. Across diverse agents on web search and deep research benchmarks, AdaCoM substantially improves performance by preserving task constraints and progress while pruning stale content. The learned strategies reveal a Fidelity-Reliability Trade-off: agents with higher vanilla ReAct performance benefit from higher-fidelity context preservation, whereas lower-performing agents require more aggressive compression to stay within a reliable reasoning regime. Transfer experiments show that AdaCoM generalizes most effectively across agents with similar capability (measured by vanilla ReAct performance), suggesting a practical path toward reusable context managers for agent systems.
2605.30785.COMMENT: Proposes AdaCoM, an external LLM trained via RL to manage context for frozen agents, enabling self-improvement through learned compression strategies.
2605.30785.title_cn: 面向长视野任务的学习代理兼容上下文管理
2605.30785.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30785v1宣布类型：新摘要：LLM代理越来越多地面临长期任务，例如网络搜索和现实世界应用程序的深入研究，其中积累的上下文可能会导致长期上下文退化和推理失败。之前的工作通过具有代理端上下文控制或总结等固定策略的上下文管理来缓解这种情况，这需要训练代理本身进行适应-这对于闭源代理来说是不切实际的，并且忽略了不同代理可能需要不同的策略。我们引入自适应上下文管理（AdaCoM），它训练外部LLM通过灵活的修改动作和端到端强化学习来管理冻结代理的上下文。在网络搜索和深度研究基准的各种代理中，AdaCoM通过保留任务约束和进度同时修剪陈旧内容，大幅提高了性能。所学到的策略揭示了一种保真度与可靠性的权衡：具有更高ReAct性能的代理从更高保真度的上下文保存中受益，而性能较低的代理需要更积极的压缩才能保持在可靠的推理机制内。传输实验表明，AdaCoM在具有相似能力的代理中最有效地推广（通过vanilla ReAct性能衡量），为代理系统提供了可重复使用的上下文管理器的实用途径。

2605.31062.title: AdaptR1: Reinforcement Learning Based Adaptive Interleaved Thinking in Multi-hop Question Answering
2605.31062.abstract: arXiv:2605.31062v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable performance in complex reasoning tasks through Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting. However, this approach often leads to ``over-thinking,'' where models generate unnecessarily long reasoning traces for simple queries and incur avoidable inference cost. While recent work has explored adaptive reasoning, existing methods typically make a single query-level decision about whether to reason. This overlooks the dynamic nature of multi-step tasks, where the need for explicit reasoning varies across intermediate stages. To address this limitation, we introduce AdaptR1, a Reinforcement Learning (RL) based framework for adaptive interleaved thinking in multi-hop Question Answering (QA). Unlike previous approaches that require Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) for cold-start initialization, AdaptR1 uses a fully RL-based strategy with a quality-gated efficiency reward to dynamically allocate reasoning budgets at each step. Under the Graph-R1 setting, AdaptR1 reduces average think tokens by 69.71\%, with a 90.35\% reduction on HotpotQA, while maintaining performance comparable to or better than standard baselines. Furthermore, our analysis reveals that overthinking in multi-hop reasoning is not uniformly distributed but occurs predominantly during the initial planning stages, highlighting the effectiveness of step-wise adaptive budget allocation.
2605.31062.COMMENT: Proposes AdaptR1, a fully RL-based framework with a quality-gated efficiency reward for adaptive step-wise reasoning budget allocation in multi-hop QA, directly addressing RL for LLMs and reasoning efficiency.
2605.31062.title_cn: AdaptR1：多跳问题回答中基于强化学习的自适应交织思维
2605.31062.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31062v1宣布类型：新摘要：通过思想链（CoT）提示，大型语言模型（LLM）在复杂推理任务中取得了出色的性能。然而，这种方法通常会导致“过度思考”，即模型为简单查询生成不必要的长推理痕迹，并产生可避免的推理成本。虽然最近的工作探索了适应性推理，但现有方法通常会做出关于是否推理的单一查询级决策。这忽视了多步骤任务的动态性质，其中对显式推理的需求在各个中间阶段有所不同。为了解决这一局限性，我们引入了AdaptR 1，这是一个基于强化学习（RL）的框架，用于多跳问题解答（QA）中的自适应交叉思维。与之前需要监督微调（SFT）进行冷启动初始化的方法不同，AdaptR 1使用完全基于RL的策略和质量门控效率奖励来动态分配每个步骤的推理预算。在Graph-R1设置下，AdaptR 1将平均思维代币减少了69.71%，HotpotQA减少了90.35%，同时保持与标准基线相当或更好的性能。此外，我们的分析表明，多跳推理中的过度思考并不是均匀分布的，而是主要发生在初始规划阶段，凸显了分步自适应预算分配的有效性。

2605.30524.title: Representation Collapse in Sequential Post-Training of Large Language Models
2605.30524.abstract: arXiv:2605.30524v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models are now adapted through chains of post-training stages rather than through a single instruction-tuning pass. This paper studies whether such sequential post-training gradually compresses internal representations into low-rank, anisotropic, and homogeneous feature spaces. We define a measurement suite for hidden states, logits, token trajectories, and LoRA updates, and we use it to analyze supervised fine-tuning, preference optimization, safety/refusal tuning, math and code specialization, and long chain-of-thought tuning under controlled stage orderings. The central hypothesis is that excessive representation concentration is not merely a geometric curiosity: it predicts reduced plasticity during later adaptation, weaker out-of-domain generalization, and poorer calibration. We further evaluate lightweight interventions, including mixed-domain replay, feature refresh, representation diversity regularization, and LoRA update decorrelation, as ways to preserve future learnability without giving up the behavioral gains of post-training.
2605.30524.COMMENT: Directly studies representation collapse in sequential RL-based post-training (including RLHF, preference optimization) and proposes interventions to preserve plasticity, which is highly relevant to RL for LLMs.
2605.30524.title_cn: 大型语言模型顺序后训练中的崩溃表示
2605.30524.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30524v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型现在可以通过一系列训练后阶段而不是通过一个单一的描述调整过程来调整。本文研究这种顺序后训练是否会逐渐将内部表示压缩到低等级、各向异性和同质特征空间中。我们为隐藏状态、逻辑、令牌轨迹和LoRA更新定义了一个测量套件，并使用它来分析有监督的微调、偏好优化、安全/拒绝调整、数学和代码专业化以及受控阶段排序下的长思想链调整。中心假设是，过度的代表集中不仅仅是一种几何好奇心：它预测了后期适应过程中可塑性下降、域外概括较弱和校准较差。我们进一步评估轻量级干预措施，包括混合域重播、特征刷新、表示多样性正规化和LoRA更新去相关，作为在不放弃训练后行为收益的情况下保留未来可学习性的方法。

2605.31075.title: Task-Focused Memorization for Multimodal Agents
2605.31075.abstract: arXiv:2605.31075v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Long-term memory is essential for multimodal agents to build coherent experience, accumulate world knowledge, and achieve continual learning. However, constructing effective memory goes beyond memory module design and basic requirements such as accuracy and fidelity; the key challenge lies in determining what to memorize. Multimodal agents, such as embodied agents, continuously perceive, reason, and act in real or virtual environments, receiving an unbounded stream of multimodal observations. From this combinatorial explosion of information, an agent must selectively retain content that is relevant to its role in the environment and valuable for future tasks. To bridge this gap, we frame memory generation as a learnable memorization policy and introduce TaskMem (Task-focused Memorization Policy Learning), a reinforcement-learning-based framework that enables the policy to dynamically adjust its focus to the demands of real tasks encountered in the environment. TaskMem adopts a two-phase training paradigm: Phase One learns how to memorize by optimizing memory quality under fundamental fidelity requirements; Phase Two occurs after deployment, where the agent learns what to memorize by tuning an adapter on its base MLLM, using recent environment tasks to define a reward model that guides the memorization policy toward task-relevant content. To evaluate our approach, we reformulate VideoMME, EgoLife, and EgoTempo into streaming benchmarks that simulate a realistic setting in which an agent processes streaming observations and handles tasks arriving online. To isolate memory assessment, the questions must be answered using only the agent's memory, without access to raw video. Built on Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B, TaskMem improves VQA accuracy by 6.3%, 7.0%, and 5.3% on these benchmarks, respectively.
2605.31075.COMMENT: Proposes a reinforcement-learning-based framework (TaskMem) for learning a memorization policy in multimodal agents, enabling self-improvement from environment interaction, which strongly matches the self-evolving agents criterion.
2605.31075.title_cn: 多模式代理的以任务为中心的并行化
2605.31075.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31075v1宣布类型：新摘要：长期记忆对于多模式智能体建立连贯的经验、积累世界知识和实现持续学习至关重要。然而，构建有效记忆超出了记忆模块设计和准确性和保真度等基本要求;关键挑战在于确定要记住什么。多模态代理，如体现代理，不断感知，推理，并在现实或虚拟环境中采取行动，接收无限流的多模态观察。从这种信息的组合爆炸中，智能体必须选择性地保留与其在环境中的角色相关并对未来任务有价值的内容。为了弥合这一差距，我们框架的记忆生成作为一个可学习的记忆策略，并引入TaskMem（以任务为中心的记忆策略学习），一个基于记忆学习的框架，使政策能够动态地调整其重点，以满足环境中遇到的实际任务的需求。TaskMem采用两阶段的训练范式：第一阶段通过在基本保真度要求下优化记忆质量来学习如何记忆;第二阶段发生在部署之后，其中代理通过在其基础MLLM上调整适配器来学习要记忆的内容，使用最近的环境任务来定义奖励模型，该模型指导记忆策略朝向任务相关内容。为了评估我们的方法，我们将VideoME、EgoLife和EgoTempo重新定义为流媒体基准，模拟现实环境，其中代理处理流媒体观察并处理到达在线的任务。为了隔离记忆评估，必须仅使用代理的记忆来回答问题，而不能访问原始视频。TaskMem基于Qwen 3-DL-30 B-A3 B构建，在这些基准测试上分别将VQA准确性提高了6.3%、7.0%和5.3%。

2605.30727.title: MosaicLeaks:Privacy Risks in Querying-in-the-Open for Deep Research Agents
2605.30727.abstract: arXiv:2605.30727v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Deep research agents increasingly combine private local documents with external tools like web retrieval, creating a privacy risk: an agent's external queries may leak sensitive information from its local context. This risk is amplified by the mosaic effect, where individual queries may appear harmless but become revealing in aggregate. We introduce MosaicLeaks, a benchmark of 1,001 multi-hop deep research tasks that chain private enterprise documents and a public web corpus, forcing agents to make external queries that depend on local information. We evaluate leakage with an adversary LLM that observes only the agent's external queries and attempts to infer private information at three levels: the agent's research intent, answers to specific private questions and verifiable claims about the enterprise documents. We find that models across families and sizes frequently leak at all three levels, that zero-shot privacy prompting reduces but does not eliminate leakage and that reinforcement learning for task performance alone worsens leakage. To address this, we propose Privacy-Aware Deep Research (PA-DR), an RL framework that combines situational rewards for task success with a learned privacy classifier to provide dense credit assignment over both per-query and mosaic-level leakage. Training Qwen3-4B-Instruct with PA-DR improves accuracy from 48.7% to 58.7% and reduces answer and full-information leakage from 34.0% to 9.9%.
2605.30727.COMMENT: Proposes a novel RL framework (PA-DR) combining task rewards with a learned privacy classifier for dense credit assignment, directly improving LLM behavior via RL.
2605.30727.title_cn: MosaicLeaks：深度研究代理公开查询中的隐私风险
2605.30727.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30727v1宣布类型：新摘要：深度研究代理越来越多地将私人本地文档与网络检索等外部工具相结合，从而产生了隐私风险：代理的外部查询可能会泄露其本地上下文中的敏感信息。马赛克效应放大了这种风险，其中单个查询可能看起来无害，但总体上变得具有揭示性。我们引入了MosaicLeaks，这是1，001个多跳深度研究任务的基准，将私营企业文档和公共网络数据库链接起来，迫使代理根据本地信息进行外部查询。我们使用对手LLM来评估泄漏，该LLM仅观察代理的外部查询，并尝试在三个层面推断私人信息：代理的研究意图、特定私人问题的答案以及有关企业文件的可验证声明。我们发现，跨系列和规模的模型经常在所有三个级别上发生泄漏，零曝光隐私提示可以减少但无法消除泄漏，而单独用于任务性能的强化学习可以加剧泄漏。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了隐私感知深度研究（PA-DR），这是一个RL框架，它将任务成功的情景奖励与习得的隐私分类器相结合，以在每次查询和马赛克级泄露方面提供密集的信用分配。使用PA-DR培训Qwen 3 - 4 B-Direct将准确率从48.7%提高到58.7%，并将答案和完整信息泄露从34.0%降低到9.9%。

2605.31271.title: DriveMA: Driving Vision-Language-Action Models with verifiable Meta-Actions
2605.31271.abstract: arXiv:2605.31271v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Driving Vision-Language-Action Models (Driving VLAs) aim to use language to improve end-to-end planning, but the language-action gap limits this promise. We propose DriveMA, a Driving VLA framework built on verifiable meta-actions, which summarize future ego motion into compact language-domain intentions and can be constructed from expert trajectories with a trajectory-grounded annotation pipeline and can be verified against generated trajectories through rule-based projection. DriveMA exploits this verifiability with action-centric supervised training and a data-efficient turn-level credit assignment reinforcement learning framework, explicitly aligning high-level decisions with low-level trajectory planning through dense rewards and precise credit assignment. DriveMA sets a new state of the art on the Waymo Open Dataset Vision-based E2E Driving, achieving a Rater Feedback Score of 8.060 with a 2B model and further improving it to 8.079 with a 4B model; it also obtains competitive closed-loop planning performance on NAVSIM. These results show that even a simple meta-action interface can achieve state-of-the-art planning when made verifiable and optimized for language-action alignment. Code, data, and models will be released to facilitate future research.
2605.31271.COMMENT: Proposes a Driving VLA framework with verifiable meta-actions and a turn-level credit assignment RL pipeline for aligning high-level decisions with low-level planning.
2605.31271.title_cn: DriveMA：通过可验证的元动作驱动视觉-语言-动作模型
2605.31271.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31271v1宣布类型：新摘要：驱动视觉-语言-动作模型（驱动VLA）旨在使用语言来改善端到端规划，但语言-动作的差距限制了这一承诺。我们提出了DriveMA，这是一个基于可验证元动作的驱动VLA框架，它将未来的自我运动总结为紧凑的语言领域意图，并且可以通过基于知识的注释管道从专家轨迹构建，并且可以通过基于规则的投影针对生成的轨迹进行验证。DriveMA通过以行动为中心的监督训练和数据高效的回合级信用分配强化学习框架来利用这种可验证性，通过密集奖励和精确的信用分配明确地将高层决策与低级轨迹规划保持一致。DriveMA在Waymo Open Dataset基于Vision的E2 E驾驶方面开创了新的最新水平，2B模型的评分者反馈评分达到8.060，4 B模型的评分者反馈评分进一步提高至8.079;它还在NAVSIM上获得了有竞争力的闭环规划性能。这些结果表明，即使是简单的元动作界面，只要经过验证并优化语言与动作的一致性，也可以实现最先进的规划。将发布代码、数据和模型以促进未来的研究。

2605.30903.title: Inverse Reinforcement Learning without an Optimal Demonstrator: A Feasible Reward Set Approach
2605.30903.abstract: arXiv:2605.30903v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) typically assumes demonstrations from a single optimal demonstrator, but in many applications data come from multiple imperfect demonstrators with heterogeneous suboptimality levels. We study reward learning in this setting through a feasible-reward-set framework: for each demonstrator, we encode its declared suboptimality level as a linear constraint and intersect the resulting feasible sets across demonstrators. Our theoretical analysis shows that the joint feasible set shrinks monotonically as data are added, and we give an exact characterization of when a new demonstrator strictly tightens it. We further establish two recovery guarantees for the feasible reward set of the ground-truth optimal demonstrator: one bound depends on closeness to the optimal occupancy, while the other requires only sufficient coverage and no near-optimal demonstrator. On the practical side, we introduce strategies to address the inherent reward ambiguity in the obtained reward set and provide an offline algorithm with function approximation for high-dimensional environments. Experiments in tabular grid-world and large language model (LLM) fine-tuning settings are consistent with the theoretical predictions and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework over baselines.
2605.30903.COMMENT: Proposes a novel feasible-reward-set IRL framework for learning from multiple suboptimal demonstrators, with theoretical guarantees and application to LLM fine-tuning, directly contributing to RL for LLMs.
2605.30903.title_cn: 没有最佳演示者的反向强化学习：一种可行的奖励集方法
2605.30903.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30903v1宣布类型：新摘要：反向强化学习（IRL）通常假设来自单个最优演示者的演示，但在许多应用程序中，数据来自具有不同次优水平的多个不完美演示者。我们通过可行奖励集框架研究这种环境下的奖励学习：对于每个演示者，我们将其宣布的次优水平编码为线性约束，并在演示者之间交叉产生的可行集。我们的理论分析表明，随着数据的添加，联合可行集单调缩小，并且我们给出了新演示者何时严格收紧它的精确描述。我们进一步为地面真相最优演示者的可行奖励集建立了两个恢复保证：一个界限取决于接近最优占用率，而另一个界限只需要足够的覆盖范围而不需要接近最优的演示者。在实践方面，我们引入了策略来解决所获得的奖励集中固有的奖励模糊性，并为多维环境提供具有函数逼近的离线算法。表格网格世界和大型语言模型（LLM）微调设置中的实验与理论预测一致，并证明了所提出框架在基线上的有效性。

2605.30859.title: DARTS: Distribution-Aware Active Rollout Trajectory Shaping for Accelerating LLM Reinforcement Learning
2605.30859.abstract: arXiv:2605.30859v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has become pivotal for improving model capabilities yet suffers from rollout efficiency bottlenecks due to the long-tail response length distribution. While existing works mitigate the impact of long tails via prompt-level tail scheduling, we focus on the root source of inefficiency: the distribution itself. Specifically, we characterize the long-tail distribution at a finer granularity, identifying intra-prompt long tails, and revealing that they frequently consist of ineffective verbosity. To address this, we propose a novel paradigm of active distribution shaping to shape the rollout distribution towards conciseness and certainty, thereby fundamentally resolving tail-induced overheads. We achieve this through a distribution-aware trajectory sampling mechanism, which selects trajectories from a redundant exploration space for each prompt, and an adaptive redundancy allocation scheme to maximize both shaping effectiveness and system efficiency. Experiments demonstrate significant acceleration over state-of-the-art systems by up to 1.77x without compromising model performance.
2605.30859.COMMENT: Proposes a distribution-aware active rollout trajectory shaping method to accelerate LLM RL training, directly addressing a key efficiency bottleneck in RL for LLMs.
2605.30859.title_cn: DARTS：分布感知的主动滚动轨迹成形，用于加速LLM强化学习
2605.30859.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30859v1宣布类型：新摘要：强化学习（RL）已成为提高模型能力的关键，但由于长尾响应长度分布而面临推出效率瓶颈。虽然现有的作品通过预算级尾部调度减轻了长尾的影响，但我们关注的是效率低下的根源：分发本身。具体来说，我们以更细的粒度来描述长尾分布，识别提示内长尾，并揭示它们通常由无效的冗长组成。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新的主动分布整形范式，以使推出分布变得简洁和确定，从而从根本上解决尾部引起的管理费用。我们通过分布感知轨迹采样机制来实现这一目标，该机制为每个提示从冗余探索空间中选择轨迹，以及自适应冗余分配方案，以最大限度地提高成形有效性和系统效率。实验证明，在不影响模型性能的情况下，与最先进的系统相比，可显着加速高达1.77倍。

2605.31034.title: Annealed Softmax Greedy in Many-Armed Bayesian Bandits
2605.31034.abstract: arXiv:2605.31034v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and group-based policy optimization methods such as GRPO update a stochastic policy by sampling multiple completions per prompt and increasing the policy's probability on those with higher reward, regularized by a KL penalty toward a reference policy. These updates do not include explicit mechanisms that track epistemic uncertainty. This paper studies a stylized explanation for why such uncertainty-agnostic updates can nevertheless be effective. We analyze an annealed softmax (Boltzmann) policy that selects actions according to a softmax of empirical mean rewards in a many-armed Bayesian Bernoulli bandit. Under a linear upper-tail condition on the prior (the $\beta=1$ case of $\beta$-regularity), which implies an abundance of near-optimal arms, we prove that annealed softmax greedy achieves Bayes regret $\tilde{O}(m + T/m)$, and in particular $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ when the number of arms scales as $m = \Theta(\sqrt{T})$. This is the near-optimal Bayes regret rate in this regime, attained also by empirical-mean greedy. Under $\beta$-regularity, many arms maintain empirical means close to the optimum throughout learning, so when softmax samples an arm other than the empirically best, that arm tends to be another near-optimal one rather than a clearly inferior one. By contrast, with a small number of arms, the same kind of softmax policy can suffer linear regret. The result also provides a structural analogy to RLVR, where a base policy with a non-negligible probability of producing a correct completion plays the role of $\beta$-regularity.
2605.31034.COMMENT: Provides a theoretical analysis of uncertainty-agnostic RL updates (like GRPO) in many-armed bandits, offering a structural analogy to RLVR for LLMs.
2605.31034.title_cn: 多武装贝氏强盗中的Annealed Softmax贪婪
2605.31034.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31034v1宣布类型：新摘要：具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）和基于组的策略优化方法（例如GRPO）通过对每个提示进行多个完成采样来更新随机策略，并增加具有更高奖励的策略的概率，并通过KL惩罚对参考策略进行正规化。这些更新不包括跟踪认识不确定性的明确机制。本文研究了为什么这种不确定性不可知的更新仍然有效的程序化解释。我们分析了一个经过修改的softmax（Boltzmann）策略，该策略根据多武装Bayesian Bernoulli强盗的经验平均回报的softmax选择行动。在先验的线性上尾条件（$\Beta$-regulatory的$\Beta=1$情况）下，这意味着大量的接近最优的手臂，我们证明了，当手臂数量按比例缩放为$m = \Theta（\SQRT{T}）$时，经过深思熟虑的softmax贪婪实现了Bayes后悔$\tilde {O}（m + T/m）$，特别是$\tilde{O}（\SQRT {T}）$。这是该制度中近乎最优的Bayes后悔率，也是通过商业平均贪婪获得的。在$\beta$-正则性下，许多手臂在整个学习过程中保持接近最优的经验均值，因此当softmax对经验最优之外的手臂进行采样时，该手臂往往是另一个接近最优的手臂，而不是明显较差的手臂。相比之下，在武器数量较少的情况下，同样的softmax策略可能会遭受线性遗憾。结果还提供了一个结构类比RLVR，其中一个基本的政策与不可忽略的概率产生一个正确的完成发挥作用的$\beta$-规律性。

2605.31509.title: Skill Reuse as Compression in Agentic RL
2605.31509.abstract: arXiv:2605.31509v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning (RL) often learn brittle, task-specific shortcuts. We hypothesize that agents generalize better when their successful trajectories are structurally compressible, decomposed into a small set of reusable abstract patterns. To formalize this, we introduce ReuseRL, which grounds agentic RL in the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle. ReuseRL extracts a shared skill dictionary from successful trajectories and augments the RL objective with a segmentation cost, explicitly penalizing idiosyncratic behaviors that encode poorly. We prove a PAC-Bayes generalization bound for this compression penalty. Across ALFWorld, TextWorld-Cooking, and Countdown-Stepwise, ReuseRL improves in- and out-of-distribution success over vanilla GRPO and strong round-length baselines.
2605.31509.COMMENT: Proposes ReuseRL, a framework that grounds agentic RL in the MDL principle by extracting reusable skill dictionaries from trajectories, directly addressing self-improving agents via compression.
2605.31509.title_cn: 在Atltic RL中作为压缩的技能重用
2605.31509.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31509v1宣布类型：新摘要：接受强化学习（RL）训练的大型语言模型代理通常会学习脆弱的、特定于任务的捷径。我们假设，当代理人的成功轨迹在结构上是可压缩的、分解成一小群可重复使用的抽象模式时，它们会更好地概括。为了将其形式化，我们引入了ReuseRL，它以最小描述长度（MDL）原则为基础。ReuseRL从成功的轨迹中提取共享技能字典，并通过分段成本来增强RL目标，明确惩罚编码不佳的独特行为。我们证明了这种压缩惩罚的Pac-Bayes推广界。在ALFWorld、TextWorld-Cooking和Countdown-Stepwise中，ReuseRL比香草GRPO和强大的圆形基线提高了分发内和分发外的成功率。

2605.30712.title: ExpGraph: Model-Agnostic Experience Learning with Graph-Structured Memory for LLM Agents
2605.30712.abstract: arXiv:2605.30712v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents have shown strong capabilities in reasoning, tool use, and multi-step interaction, but they often solve tasks from scratch and fail to reuse successful strategies or failure lessons from prior experience. Fine-tuning on collected experience can improve reuse, but it is inflexible when stronger or more suitable executors emerge. We propose ExpGraph, a model-agnostic experience learning framework that enables frozen and replaceable LLM executors to improve through external experience reuse without parameter updates. ExpGraph summarizes historical trajectories into reusable skills and failure lessons, organizes them as nodes in a self-evolving experience graph, and retrieves useful experiences through graph diffusion and utility-aware ranking. A lightweight retrieval copilot is trained with reinforcement learning using feedback that compares executor performance with and without retrieved experiences, while the graph is updated online from downstream task outcomes. We evaluate ExpGraph on ExpSuite, covering question answering, mathematical reasoning, code generation, and multi-step agentic environments including ALFWorld and AppWorld. ExpGraph improves over the strongest baseline by 12.2% and 4.7% on static tasks with smaller and larger executors, and by 21.4% and 12.7% in agentic environments, while reducing average interaction steps by 12.7% and 21.6%. Ablations show that graph-structured experience, utility-aware ranking, and adaptive retrieval jointly enable effective experience reuse across diverse tasks and executor models.
2605.30712.COMMENT: Proposes a model-agnostic experience learning framework with a self-evolving graph-structured memory and RL-trained retrieval copilot for LLM agents, enabling closed-loop improvement.
2605.30712.title_cn: ExpShape：针对LLM代理的具有图形结构化内存的模型不可知体验学习
2605.30712.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30712v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）代理在推理、工具使用和多步骤交互方面表现出了强大的能力，但它们通常从头开始解决任务，并且无法重用成功的策略或之前经验中的失败教训。对收集的经验进行微调可以提高重用性，但当出现更强大或更合适的执行者时，它是不灵活的。我们提出了ExpShape，这是一个模型不可知的体验学习框架，它使冻结和可替换的LLM执行者能够通过外部体验重用进行改进，而无需更新参数。ExpShape将历史轨迹总结为可重复使用的技能和失败教训，将它们组织为自我进化的体验图中的节点，并通过图扩散和效用感知排名来检索有用的体验。轻量级检索副驾驶员通过强化学习进行训练，使用反馈比较执行者在有和没有检索到的经验的情况下的表现，同时根据下游任务结果在线更新图表。我们在ExpSuite上评估ExpShape，涵盖问题回答、数学推理、代码生成以及包括ALFWorld和AppWorld在内的多步骤代理环境。在执行者越来越小的静态任务中，ExpShape比最强基线提高了12.2%和4.7%，在代理环境中提高了21.4%和12.7%，同时将平均交互步骤减少了12.7%和21.6%。简化表明，图形结构体验、效用感知排名和自适应检索共同实现了在不同任务和执行者模型之间的有效体验重用。

2605.31492.title: LinTree: Improving LLM Reasoning with Explicitly Structured Search Histories
2605.31492.abstract: arXiv:2605.31492v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often solve reasoning problems by generating intermediate traces that explore and revise partial solutions. From a search perspective, these traces can be viewed as linearized search trees, where the model extends a partial solution, abandons it when it fails, and backtracks to try alternatives. Compared with traditional heuristic-guided search, such a policy has a potential advantage: it conditions on the whole search trace rather than only on the current local state. We first test whether LLMs utilize this advantage by comparing trace-conditioned reasoning policies against best-first search equipped with an LLM heuristic that only observes the current local state. Across three controlled reasoning environments, Blocks World, grid Navigation, and Sokoban, we find that raw access to search history alone is not enough to reliably outperform heuristic search. We then study one possible reason: in LLM reasoning traces, the underlying search tree is only implicitly represented, and when the model backtracks or switches branches, the trace does not explicitly identify which earlier search state is being revisited. We show that adding simple parent pointers to explicitly represent the linearized tree (LinTree) structure improves both task performance and search efficiency relative to implicit reasoning models and LLM-heuristic-guided search. These results suggest that search history becomes most useful when its tree structure is made explicit, motivating more structure-aware representations for LLM reasoning.
2605.31492.COMMENT: Proposes LinTree, a method to explicitly represent the tree structure of search histories in LLM reasoning traces, improving reasoning performance and efficiency over implicit representations.
2605.31492.title_cn: LinTree：利用显式结构化搜索历史改进LLM推理
2605.31492.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31492v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）通常通过生成探索和修改部分解决方案的中间轨迹来解决推理问题。从搜索的角度来看，这些痕迹可以被视为线性化搜索树，其中模型扩展了部分解决方案，在失败时放弃它，并倒退尝试替代方案。与传统的启发式搜索相比，这种政策具有潜在的优势：它以整个搜索轨迹为条件，而不仅仅是当前的当地州。我们首先通过将轨迹条件推理策略与配备仅观察当前局部状态的LLM启发式的最佳优先搜索进行比较来测试LLM是否利用了这一优势。在Blocks World、grid Navigator和Sokoban这三个受控推理环境中，我们发现仅对搜索历史的原始访问不足以可靠地优于启发式搜索。然后我们研究一个可能的原因：在LLM推理轨迹中，底层搜索树仅被隐式表示，当模型回溯或切换分支时，轨迹不会显式识别正在重新访问哪个早期搜索状态。我们表明，相对于隐式推理模型和LLM启发式引导搜索，添加简单的父指针来显式表示线性化树（LinTree）结构可以提高任务性能和搜索效率。这些结果表明，当搜索历史的树结构变得显式时，搜索历史变得最有用，从而激励LLM推理的更多结构感知表示。

2605.31159.title: Trust-Region Behavior Blending for On-Policy Distillation
2605.31159.abstract: arXiv:2605.31159v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student on prefixes sampled from its own policy while matching a stronger teacher. This addresses the prefix mismatch of offline distillation, but early student rollouts can still be poor, placing teacher supervision on weak or low-quality prefixes. We propose Trust-Region behavior Blending (TRB), a warmup method that replaces the early rollout policy with the closest-to-teacher behavior policy inside a student-centered KL trust region, while keeping the per-prefix reverse-KL OPD loss unchanged. The KL budget is annealed to zero, so training returns to pure student rollouts after warmup. Across two math-reasoning distillation settings, TRB attains the strongest average among the compared methods.
2605.31159.COMMENT: Proposes Trust-Region behavior Blending (TRB), a novel warmup method for on-policy distillation that uses a KL trust region to improve student rollout quality, directly relevant to RL for LLMs.
