2605.31159.title_cn: 信托区域行为融合以实现政策上的蒸馏
2605.31159.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31159v1宣布类型：新摘要：按政策蒸馏（OPD）对学生进行从自己的政策中抽样的前置培训，同时匹配更强大的教师。这解决了离线蒸馏的前置不匹配问题，但早期的学生推广仍然可能很差，导致教师监督薄弱或低质量的前置。我们提出了信任区域行为混合（TRB），这是一种热身方法，在以学生为中心的KL信任区域内，用最接近教师的行为政策取代早期推出政策，同时保持每个前置反向KL OPD损失不变。KL预算已降至零，因此训练在热身后恢复为纯学生。在两种数学推理蒸馏设置中，TRB在比较方法中获得了最强的平均值。

2605.31058.title: Combinatorial Synthesis: Scaling Code RLVR via Atomic Decomposition and Recombination
2605.31058.abstract: arXiv:2605.31058v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has recently emerged as the cornerstone for shaping the remarkable coding abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, the scalability of RLVR is severely constrained by the scarcity of sufficiently challenging verifiable code tasks that target near the model's edge of competence. Prior studies often rely on heuristic seed expansions for data synthesis, which severely limits both novelty and difficulty. Consequently, the training value of such data fails to scale proportionally with the size of its synthesis. To this end, we propose Atomic Decomposition and Recombination (ADR), a novel framework that generates verifiable code tasks via decomposition into atomic elements and controlled recombination, thereby enabling the generation of genuinely novel and challenging verifiable code tasks. Experiments and analysis demonstrate that ADR achieves superior originality, difficulty, diversity, and test quality over existing baselines, and consistently delivers greater improvements in code ability across RLVR in diverse downstream domains, including algorithmic programming, tool usage, and data science. Our work sheds light on a new paradigm for novel code task synthesis and scalable RLVR training.
2605.31058.COMMENT: Proposes Atomic Decomposition and Recombination (ADR), a novel framework for generating verifiable code tasks to scale RLVR training for LLMs.
2605.31058.title_cn: 组合综合：基于原子分解和原子简化的尺度码RLVR
2605.31058.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31058v1宣布类型：新摘要：带可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）最近已成为塑造大型语言模型（LLM）非凡编码能力的基石。然而，由于缺乏针对接近模型能力边缘的具有足够挑战性的可验证代码任务，RL VR的可扩展性受到严重限制。之前的研究通常依赖于启发式种子扩展来进行数据合成，这严重限制了新颖性和难度。因此，此类数据的训练值未能与其合成的大小成比例地缩放。为此，我们提出了原子分解和分解（ADR），这是一种新颖的框架，通过分解为原子元素和受控重组来生成可验证的代码任务，从而能够生成真正新颖且具有挑战性的可验证代码任务。实验和分析表明，ADR比现有基线实现了更高的原创性、难度、多样性和测试质量，并在不同下游领域（包括算法编程、工具使用和数据科学）中持续为整个WLVR的代码能力提供更大的改进。我们的工作揭示了新型代码任务合成和可扩展的WLVR训练的新范式。

2605.30487.title: Configurable Reward Model for Balanced Safety Alignment
2605.30487.abstract: arXiv:2605.30487v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Aligning large language models (LLMs) to heterogeneous and rapidly evolving safety requirements remains a critical challenge. Existing instruction-tuned LLMs and standalone safety classifiers often fail to generalize to new safety configurations, motivating the need for Reward Models (RMs) that are explicitly configurable to changing specifications. We introduce the Configurable Safety Reward Model (CSRM), which is jointly optimized for calibrated safety compliance and reward modeling. Our approach is supported by configuration-targeted data augmentation that enforces instruction adherence while preserving relative severity structure. The resulting RM is sensitive to fine-grained safety configurations and conversational nuances, substantially improving generalization to previously unseen safety configurations. CSRM achieves state-of-the-art performance on recent configurable safety benchmarks, including CoSApien (94.6% F1) and DynaBench (75.8% F1), without requiring additional human annotation. When used for downstream safety alignment, CSRM yields LLMs with a significantly improved helpfulness-safety tradeoff compared to existing baselines.
2605.30487.COMMENT: Proposes a configurable safety reward model (CSRM) for RL-based alignment, directly addressing reward model design for scalable safety alignment.
2605.30487.title_cn: 平衡安全调整的可配置奖励模型
2605.30487.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30487v1宣布类型：新摘要：使大型语言模型（LLM）与异类且快速发展的安全要求保持一致仍然是一项严峻的挑战。现有的经过优化的LLM和独立的安全分类器通常无法推广到新的安全配置，从而引发了对可针对不断变化的规范明确配置的奖励模型（RM）的需求。我们引入了可配置安全奖励模型（CRM），该模型针对校准的安全合规性和奖励建模进行了联合优化。我们的方法得到了针对配置的数据增强的支持，该数据增强在保留相对严重性结构的同时强制执行指令遵守性。由此产生的RM对细粒度的安全配置和对话细微差别敏感，大大提高了对以前从未见过的安全配置的概括性。CRM在最近的可配置安全基准（包括CoSApien（94.6% F1）和DynaBench（75.8% F1））上实现了最先进的性能，无需额外的人类注释。当用于下游安全对齐时，CRM产生的LLM与现有基线相比，帮助与安全权衡显着改善。

2605.31545.title: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for Personalized Evaluation
2605.31545.abstract: arXiv:2605.31545v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) evolve from general-purpose assistants to user-centric agents, personalization has become central to aligning model behavior with individual preferences, making the evaluation of personalized alignment a critical bottleneck. Existing evaluation methods-ranging from automatic metrics to LLM-as-a-judge approaches-fail to capture subjective, user-specific preferences embedded in long-term interaction histories. We identify three essential principles for reliable and effective personalized evaluation: Representativeness, User-Consistency, and Discriminativeness. To address these principles, we introduce Personalized Evaluation as Learning, a paradigm that formulates personalized evaluation as a learning problem rather than a static judgment. Under this paradigm, we propose PARL (Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for Personalized Evaluation), a framework that learns to induce preference-aware evaluation rubrics directly from raw user histories and performs a self-validation mechanism to ensure consistency with the user's preferences. PARL integrates rubric induction with a discriminative reinforcement learning objective that contrasts user-authored responses against competitive personalized model outputs, enabling the learned rubrics to capture precise, user-specific decision boundaries. Experiments on real-world personalized text generation tasks show that PARL consistently induces high-fidelity rubrics that reliably identify user-aligned responses and generalize across users and tasks, while capturing stable stylistic preferences and fine-grained evaluative patterns. To ensure reproducibility, our code is available at https://github.com/SnowCharmQ/PARL.
2605.31545.COMMENT: Proposes a new RL-based rubric learning framework for personalized evaluation of LLMs, directly matching RL for LLMs via a discriminative RL objective.
2605.31545.title_cn: 用于个性化评估的偏好感知版块学习
2605.31545.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31545v1公告类型：新摘要：随着大型语言模型（LLM）从通用助手发展到以用户为中心的代理，个性化已经成为将模型行为与个人偏好相匹配的核心，使得个性化匹配的评估成为关键瓶颈。现有的评估方法，从自动指标到法学硕士作为一个判断的方法，未能捕捉到主观的，用户特定的偏好嵌入在长期的互动历史。我们确定了可靠有效的个性化评估的三个基本原则：代表性、用户一致性和区分性。为了解决这些原则，我们引入了个性化评估即学习，这是一种将个性化评估制定为学习问题而不是静态判断的范式。在这个范式下，我们提出了PARL（偏好感知的个性化评估条目学习），这是一个学习直接从原始用户历史中引入偏好感知的评估条目的框架，并执行自我验证机制以确保与用户偏好的一致性。PARL将标题归纳与区分性强化学习目标集成，该目标将用户撰写的响应与竞争性的个性化模型输出进行比较，使学习的标题能够捕捉精确的、特定于用户的决策边界。对现实世界个性化文本生成任务的实验表明，PARL始终引入高保真度指标，可以可靠地识别与用户一致的响应并在用户和任务之间进行概括，同时捕捉稳定的文体偏好和细粒度的评估模式。为了确保可重复性，我们的代码可在https://github.com/SnowCharmQ/PARL上获取。

2605.31145.title: FOCUS: Forcing In-Context Object Localization through Visual Support Constraints and Policy Optimization
2605.31145.abstract: arXiv:2605.31145v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In-context localization (ICL) seeks to localize a target object specified by a small set of support examples in a query image, operating on the fly without training or parameter updates. Despite rapid advances in vision-language models (VLMs), achieving category-agnostic and visually grounded ICL remains an open problem, even though it is essential for applications such as image editing, personalized visual search, and retrieval. Existing methods are fragile and rely on explicit category supervision, which not only limits applicability in realistic settings with unnamed or instance-specific objects but also introduces category bias that steers predictions toward semantic priors rather than visual evidence. We introduce a two-stage training framework that explicitly optimizes in-context attention between support bounding boxes and query images without category supervision. We further refine localization via reinforcement learning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to directly minimize localization error. This formulation enforces visual correspondence over semantic priors, yielding robust instance-level localization. Empirically, a 7B-parameter model trained with our objectives outperforms models up to 72B parameters, demonstrating that context-aware localization objectives can surpass scaling alone. Comprehensive ablations validate the contribution of each component.
2605.31145.COMMENT: Uses GRPO (RL) to directly minimize localization error in VLMs, a strong match for RL for LLMs with a novel reward design.
2605.31145.title_cn: 焦点：通过视觉支持约束和策略优化强制上下文对象本地化
2605.31145.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31145v1公告类型：新摘要：上下文内本地化（ICL）旨在本地化由查询图像中的一小组支持示例指定的目标对象，无需训练或参数更新即可即时操作。尽管视觉语言模型（VLM）取得了迅速的进步，但实现类别不可知且基于视觉的ICL仍然是一个悬而未决的问题，尽管它对于图像编辑、个性化视觉搜索和检索等应用程序至关重要。现有的方法很脆弱，并且依赖于显式的类别监督，这不仅限制了在具有未命名或特定实例对象的现实环境中的适用性，而且还会引入类别偏见，将预测引导到语义先验而不是视觉证据。我们引入了一个两阶段训练框架，该框架显式优化支持边界框和查询图像之间的上下文注意力，而无需类别监督。我们使用群组相对政策优化（GRPO）通过强化学习进一步完善本地化，以直接最大限度地减少本地化错误。这种公式在语义先验上强制实现视觉对应，从而产生强大的实例级本地化。从经验上看，用我们的目标训练的7 B参数模型的性能优于高达72 B参数的模型，这表明上下文感知本地化目标可以超越单独的扩展。全面消融验证了每个组件的贡献。

2605.30914.title: Automating Formal Verification with Reinforcement Learning and Recursive Inference
2605.30914.abstract: arXiv:2605.30914v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Automated formal verification remains challenging for large language models because data for proof assistants and verification-aware languages is scarce, and correctness depends on satisfying precise machine-checkable specifications rather than producing plausible code. This thesis studies how verifier environments can improve LLM generation of verified programs and proofs through reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) and verifier-guided inference-time search. First, we train open-source models in Dafny with RLVR using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and related variants, assembling generated candidates into complete programs and scoring them with compiler and verifier outcomes. Initial experiments on an APPS-derived Dafny dataset increased verified reward from 2.2% to 58.1%, but revealed specification hacking, where models exploit weak formal specifications instead of implementing the intended solutions. After filtering underspecified and vulnerable tasks, multi-turn RLVR on the refined benchmark improves the verified pass rate from 9.7% to 31.1%. Second, we develop a verifier-guided inference scaffold in Lean that treats proof generation as structured search over decomposed subgoals, verifier feedback, diagnostics, and repair. With a fixed base model, the full scaffold with proof reviser improves pass rate on an initial VeriCoding pilot set from 46.2% under direct repair to 69.2%. On the larger VERINA dataset, whole-task decomposition plus proof reviser solves 7 of 42 previously unsolved tasks. We also introduce Dalek-Bench, a repository-scale Lean benchmark derived from the Rust $\texttt{curve25519-dalek}$ verification project; preliminary results remain weak, indicating that stronger progress evaluation and task-specific tool-use policies are still needed.
2605.30914.COMMENT: Proposes RLVR with GRPO for automated formal verification, addressing specification hacking and verifier-guided inference, directly matching RL for LLMs.
2605.30914.title_cn: 基于强化学习和递归推理的形式验证自动化
2605.30914.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30914v1宣布类型：新摘要：自动化形式验证对于大型语言模型来说仍然具有挑战性，因为证明助手和验证感知语言的数据稀缺，并且正确性取决于满足精确的机器可检查规范，而不是产生看似合理的代码。本论文研究验证者环境如何通过来自可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）和验证者引导的推断时搜索来改进已验证程序和证明的LLM生成。首先，我们使用组相对政策优化（GRPO）和相关变体在Dafny中训练开源模型，将生成的候选项组装到完整的程序中，并使用编译器和验证器结果对它们进行评分。对源自APPS的Dafny数据集进行的初步实验将验证奖励从2.2%提高到58.1%，但揭示了规范黑客攻击，即模型利用弱的形式规范，而不是实现预期的解决方案。在过滤未指定和脆弱的任务后，改进后的基准上的多回合WLVR将验证通过率从9.7%提高到31.1%。其次，我们在精益中开发了一个验证者引导的推理框架，它将证据生成视为对分解子目标、验证者反馈、诊断和修复的结构化搜索。通过固定基础模型，带验证修订器的完整支架将初始VeriCoding试点集的通过率从直接修复下的46.2%提高到69.2%。在更大的VERINA数据集上，整个任务分解加上证明修订器解决了42个之前未解决的任务中的7个。我们还引入了Dalek-Bench，这是一个源自Rust $\textttt {curve 25519-dalek}$验证项目的仓库规模精益基准;初步结果仍然薄弱，这表明仍然需要更强有力的进度评估和特定任务的工具使用政策。

2605.30711.title: SAGE: A Novelty Gate for Efficient Memory Evolution in Agentic LLMs
2605.30711.abstract: arXiv:2605.30711v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Agentic LLMs must continuously decide whether newly extracted facts should be added, merged with existing memories, or ignored, yet prior work has focused more on retrieval and storage than on principled write-side control. We frame memory evolution as a novelty-detection problem and propose SAGE, a Spherical Adaptive Gate for memory Evolution that scores candidate facts with a von Mises-Fisher-based density estimator over memory embeddings and routes them with an adaptive threshold that tracks memory-store geometry. SAGE resolves clearly novel facts as ADD, clearly redundant facts as NOOP, and sends only uncertain cases to an LLM merge step, reducing expensive write-time reasoning. On LoCoMo, SAGE achieves the best average token-F1 against Mem0 on all seven open-weight backbone comparisons, while on GPT-4o-mini it reduces add-phase API cost by 3.4$\times$ and add-phase latency by 2.5$\times$ with only a small average judge-score gap. As a drop-in binary gate for A-Mem, SAGE skips roughly 16-18% of LLM calls across five models with minimal quality change on open-weight backbones. These results suggest that novelty-aware write control is a practical lever for improving both memory quality and system efficiency in long-term agentic memory.
2605.30711.COMMENT: Proposes a novelty-gated memory evolution mechanism for agentic LLMs that uses density estimation to decide when to add, merge, or ignore new facts, enabling self-improvement via efficient write-side control.
2605.30711.title_cn: SAGE：大型LLM中高效内存进化的新奇之门
2605.30711.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30711v1宣布类型：新摘要：抽象的LLM必须不断决定是否应该添加、与现有记忆合并或忽略新提取的事实，但之前的工作更多地关注于检索和存储，而不是原则性的写入端控制。我们将内存进化框架为一个新颖性检测问题，并提出SAGE，这是一种用于内存进化的球形自适应门，它使用基于von Mises-Fisher的密度估计器对候选事实进行评分，并使用跟踪内存存储几何形状的自适应阈值来路由它们。SAGE将明显新颖的事实分解为ADD，将明显多余的事实分解为NOOP，并仅将不确定的案例发送到LLM合并步骤，从而减少了昂贵的写入时推理。在LoCoMo上，SAGE在所有七个开重主干比较中与Mem 0相比实现了最佳平均token-F1，而在GPT-4 o-mini上，它将添加阶段API成本降低了3.4 $\乘$，将添加阶段延迟降低了2.5 $\乘$，平均判断分数差距很小。作为A-Mem的插入式二进制门，SAGE跳过了五种模型中大约16-18%的LLM呼叫，开量级主干的质量变化最小。这些结果表明，新奇感写控制是提高长期代理记忆中的记忆质量和系统效率的实用杠杆。

2605.30838.title: COMPASS: Cognitive MCTS-Guided Process Alignment for Safe Search Agents
2605.30838.abstract: arXiv:2605.30838v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM-powered search agents enable multi-step reasoning and tool use. However, these capabilities introduce retrieval-induced safety degradation, as harmful intents may decompose into seemingly innocuous sub-queries that lead to unsafe outcomes. Existing alignment methods struggle to capture sparse safety signals and fail to supervise diverse violations across multi-step interactions. We propose COMPASS, a Cognitive MCTS-Guided Process Alignment framework designed to achieve robust safety alignment throughout the agent workflow while preserving general utility. COMPASS integrates cognitive tree exploration (CTE) to efficiently synthesize stealthy attack trajectories, and introspective step-wise alignment (ISA) to isolate risky intermediate actions for fine-grained process supervision. Empirical results show that COMPASS achieves a favorable safety-utility trade-off while requiring substantially less training data.
2605.30838.COMMENT: Proposes a cognitive MCTS-guided process alignment framework for safe LLM search agents, combining trajectory synthesis and step-wise reward supervision.
2605.30838.title_cn: COMPASS：认知MCTS引导的安全搜索代理过程对齐
2605.30838.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30838v1宣布类型：新摘要：LLM支持的搜索代理支持多步骤推理和工具使用。然而，这些功能会引入检索引起的安全性降级，因为有害意图可能会分解为看似无害的子查询，从而导致不安全的结果。现有的对齐方法难以捕捉稀疏的安全信号，并且无法在多步骤交互中监督各种违规行为。我们提出了康普斯，这是一个认知MCTS引导的流程对齐框架，旨在在整个代理工作流程中实现稳健的安全对齐，同时保持通用性。康普斯集成了认知树探索（CTE）以有效地合成隐形攻击轨迹，并集成了内省逐步对齐（ISA）以隔离有风险的中间动作以进行细粒度的流程监督。经验结果表明，COMASS实现了有利的安全与效用权衡，同时需要的训练数据少得多。

2605.30690.title: ElasticMem: Latent Memory as a Learnable Resource for LLM Agents
2605.30690.abstract: arXiv:2605.30690v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Long-term memory is essential for LLM agents to reason coherently across extended interactions, personalize responses, and reuse past experience. However, existing memory-augmented methods typically treat memory as a fixed resource: text-space approaches concatenate retrieved memories into the context window, causing substantial token overhead and sensitivity to noisy evidence, while latent-space approaches reduce textual cost but still rely on rigid retrieval or fixed-capacity memory interfaces. This creates a mismatch between query-dependent memory utility and fixed memory allocation. We propose ElasticMem, a memory-augmented LLM framework that learns to use memory as an elastic latent resource. ElasticMem builds an offline latent memory bank with retrieval keys and content caches, retrieves memories adaptively from the reasoner's hidden state, assigns each retrieved memory a variable latent budget through a learned policy, and injects selected latent states as soft memory tokens for generation. The full memory-use process is optimized with downstream task rewards through group-relative policy optimization. We evaluate ElasticMem on MemorySuite, covering memory-intensive QA and embodied agent control. Across Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct and Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct backbones, ElasticMem improves weighted average QA accuracy by 26.2% and 24.6%, and improves ALFWorld success rate by 66.3% and 27.2%, respectively, over the strongest baselines, while achieving the lowest ALFWorld token cost. Ablations and qualitative analyses further show that adaptive retrieval and elastic budget allocation help ElasticMem prioritize useful evidence and transferable plans beyond rigid cosine similarity. Our code for ElasticMem will be released at https://github.com/ulab-uiuc/ElasticMem.
2605.30690.COMMENT: Proposes ElasticMem, a latent memory framework for LLM agents that learns adaptive retrieval and variable latent budget allocation via RL, enabling self-improvement from interaction traces.
2605.30690.title_cn: ElasticMem：潜在记忆作为LLM代理的可学习资源
2605.30690.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30690v1宣布类型：新摘要：长期记忆对于LLM代理在扩展互动中连贯推理、个性化响应和重复使用过去的经验至关重要。然而，现有的存储器增强方法通常将存储器视为固定资源：文本空间方法将检索到的存储器连接到上下文窗口中，从而导致大量的令牌负担和对有噪证据的敏感性，而潜伏空间方法降低了文本成本，但仍然依赖于严格的检索或固定容量的存储器接口。这会导致查询相关内存实用程序和固定内存分配之间不匹配。我们提出了ElasticMem，这是一个内存增强的LLM框架，它学会使用内存作为弹性潜在资源。ElasticMem构建一个具有检索密钥和内容缓存的离线潜在存储器库，从推理器的隐藏状态自适应地检索存储器，通过学习的策略为每个检索到的存储器分配可变潜在预算，并将选择的潜在状态注入为软存储令牌以供生成。通过组相对策略优化，通过下游任务奖励来优化整个内存使用流程。我们在MemorySuite上评估ElasticMem，涵盖内存密集型QA和具体代理控制。在Qwen 2.5 - 3B-Direcct和Qwen 2.5 - 7 B-Direcct主干中，ElasticMem将加权平均QA准确性提高了26.2%和24.6%，将ALFWorld成功率分别提高了66.3%和27.2%，同时实现了最低的ALFWorld代币成本。消融和定性分析进一步表明，自适应检索和弹性预算分配有助于ElasticMem优先考虑有用证据和可转移计划，超越严格的cos相似性。我们的ElasticMem代码将在https://github.com/ulab-uiuc/ElasticMem上发布。

2605.31365.title: Learning to Adapt: Self-Improving Web Agent via Cognitive-Aware Exploration
2605.31365.abstract: arXiv:2605.31365v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recent advances in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have led to promising progress in web agents. However, existing web agents often rely on handcrafted execution pipelines or expensive expert trajectories, limiting their adaptability to complex, dynamic environments. To address these challenges, we propose SCALE (Self-Cognitive-Aware Learning and Exploration), which leverages three adversarial roles, Selector, Predictor, and Judger to autonomously discover the agent's limitations and expand its cognitive boundaries through environmental exploration. Moreover, we propose SCALE-Hop, a graph exploration strategy that facilitates global planning and helps agents avoid local exploration traps. To further support learning, we construct SCALE-20k, a large-scale dataset collected from 19 real-world websites, containing diverse task types and structured demonstrations generated from SCALE's exploration traces. Experimental results show that our approach significantly improves the performance and generalization of multiple MLLMs in various web environments. Our framework offers a scalable and generalizable solution for building truly autonomous and adaptive web agents.
2605.31365.COMMENT: Proposes a self-improving web agent framework (SCALE) with adversarial roles and graph exploration for autonomous cognitive expansion, directly matching the self-evolving agent criterion.
2605.31365.title_cn: 学习适应：通过认知感知探索自我改进Web代理
2605.31365.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31365v1宣布类型：新摘要：多模式大型语言模型（MLLM）的最新进展为Web代理带来了有希望的进展。然而，现有的Web代理通常依赖于手工制作的执行管道或昂贵的专家轨迹，限制了它们对复杂、动态环境的适应性。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了SCALE（自我认知感知学习和探索），它利用三个对抗角色，即预设者、预测者和判断者来自主发现主体的局限性，并通过环境探索扩展其认知边界。此外，我们提出了SCALE-Hop，这是一种图形探索策略，可以促进全球规划并帮助代理避开本地探索陷阱。为了进一步支持学习，我们构建了SCALE-20 k，这是一个从19个现实世界网站收集的大规模数据集，包含各种任务类型和根据SCALE的探索痕迹生成的结构化演示。实验结果表明，我们的方法显着提高了各种Web环境中多个MLLM的性能和通用性。我们的框架提供了一个可扩展和可通用的解决方案，用于构建真正自主和自适应的Web代理。

2605.30587.title: ReGuLaR: Relation-Grounded Latent Reasoning for Large Vision-Language Models
2605.30587.abstract: arXiv:2605.30587v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has significantly improved the reasoning ability of large vision-language models (LVLMs) by verbalizing intermediate reasoning steps in natural language. However, such discrete textual rationales are often insufficient for encoding continuous visual evidence. Recent work addresses this limitation by moving reasoning into continuous latent space. Despite promising progress, existing methods leave latent reasoning insufficiently connected to the compositional and relational structure of visual evidence. To address this gap, we introduce ReGuLaR, a relation grounded latent reasoning framework that explicitly grounds latent states in these critical yet overlooked visual evidence. ReGuLaR uses a training-time ReGFormer to focus latent reasoning on question-relevant objects and inter-object relations, while at inference time the model reasons and generates answers without invoking the ReGFormer. To support training ReGuLaR, we construct RGROUNDING-351K, a real-world vision-language dataset annotated with key object bounding boxes and inter-object relations. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks show that ReGuLaR consistently outperforms existing approaches and achieves state-of-the-art performance. We include our code in the submission and will release the code and training data publicly upon acceptance.
2605.30587.COMMENT: Proposes a novel latent reasoning framework (ReGuLaR) that grounds latent states in visual relations, directly matching the latent CoT/reasoning criterion.
2605.30587.title_cn: ReGuLaR：大型视觉语言模型的基于对象的潜在推理
2605.30587.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30587v1宣布类型：新摘要：思想链（CoT）推理通过用自然语言语言表达中间推理步骤，显着提高了大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）的推理能力。然而，这种离散的文本原理通常不足以编码连续的视觉证据。最近的工作通过将推理转移到连续的潜在空间来解决这一局限性。尽管取得了令人鼓舞的进展，但现有的方法使潜在推理与视觉证据的组成和关系结构联系不足。为了解决这一差距，我们引入了ReGuLaR，这是一个基于关系的潜在推理框架，它明确地将潜在状态建立在这些关键但被忽视的视觉证据中。ReGuLaR使用训练时ReGFormer将潜在推理集中在问题相关对象和对象间关系上，而在推理时，模型推理并生成答案，而无需调用ReGFormer。为了支持训练ReGuLaR，我们构建了RGROUNDING-351 K，这是一个用关键对象边界框和对象间关系进行注释的现实世界视觉语言数据集。跨不同基准的广泛实验表明，ReGuLaR始终优于现有方法，并实现了最先进的性能。我们将我们的代码包含在提交内容中，并在接受后公开发布代码和培训数据。

2605.30723.title: Skill is Not One-Size-Fits-All: Model-Aware Skill Alignment for LLM Agents
2605.30723.abstract: arXiv:2605.30723v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM agents increasingly retrieve externally curated skills-procedural instructions retrieved at decision time-to improve performance on long-horizon interactive tasks. Existing skill libraries are typically treated as model-agnostic, reusing the same skill formulations across backbones with substantially different capacities and behaviors. However, our controlled experiments across multiple model scales show that skill effectiveness is strongly model-dependent: a skill that benefits one backbone can harm another. Motivated by this observation, we propose MASA Model-Aware Skill Alignment, a framework that adapts skills to each target backbone without modifying agent weights. MASA operates in two stages: (1) a hierarchical skill evolution pipeline that iteratively rewrites general and task-specific skills using hill climbing and UCB-driven tree search, guided by environment feedback and model capability profiles; and (2) a lightweight model-conditioned skill rewriter trained on evolution trajectories to reproduce the adaptation in a single forward pass. Experiments across three interactive environments and four backbones show that MASA consistently achieves the best overall performance, with gains of up to 25.8 points over the strongest baseline. The learned rewriter further generalizes to unseen tasks and environments without additional search, consistently outperforming a much larger teacher LLM at a fraction of the inference cost.
2605.30723.COMMENT: Proposes a model-aware skill alignment framework for LLM agents that adapts skills to each backbone via a hierarchical evolution pipeline and a learned rewriter, enabling self-improvement from environment feedback.
2605.30723.title_cn: 技能不是万能的：LLM代理的模型感知技能对齐
2605.30723.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30723v1宣布类型：新摘要：LLM代理越来越多地检索外部策划的技能（在决策时检索的程序指令），以提高长期互动任务的绩效。现有的技能库通常被视为模型不可知的，在能力和行为截然不同的主干之间重复使用相同的技能公式。然而，我们跨多个模型尺度的对照实验表明，技能有效性强烈依赖于模型：有利于一个主干的技能可能会伤害另一个主干。受这一观察的启发，我们提出了MASA模型感知技能对齐，这是一个框架，可以在不修改代理权重的情况下调整技能以适应每个目标主干。MASA分两个阶段运作：（1）分层技能进化管道，在环境反馈和模型能力配置文件的指导下，使用爬山和UCB驱动的树搜索迭代地重写一般和特定任务的技能;和（2）轻量级的模型条件技能重写器在进化轨迹上训练，以在单次向前传递中重现适应。跨三个交互环境和四个主干的实验表明，MASA始终实现最佳的整体性能，比最强基线提高了25.8分。有学识的重写者进一步推广到看不见的任务和环境，无需额外搜索，始终以一小部分的推理成本优于更大的教师LLM。

2605.30861.title: Distilling LLM Feedback for Lean Theorem Proving
2605.30861.abstract: arXiv:2605.30861v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Post-training for reasoning models typically combines supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards, most commonly with GRPO. However, this algorithm suffers from sparse rewards, limited exploration, and mode collapse. Building upon recent works on self-distillation, we propose Feedback Distillation, a training method where the model is trained to match, at the token level, its own distribution conditioned on privileged feedback produced by a language model. Feedback Distillation offers token-level supervision and can inject external knowledge. Evaluating our method for Lean4 theorem-proving, we find that Feedback Distillation maintains greater diversity in generated trajectories than GRPO, yielding higher policy entropy and better pass@k scaling. The two methods are complementary: initializing GRPO from a Feedback Distillation checkpoint outperforms either method alone. All in all, our results suggest a promising avenue to improve post-training for complex reasoning.
2605.30861.COMMENT: Proposes Feedback Distillation, a novel RL training method that uses token-level supervision from LLM feedback to improve reasoning diversity and policy entropy, outperforming GRPO for theorem proving.
2605.30861.title_cn: 提取LLM反馈以证明精益定理
2605.30861.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30861v1公告类型：新摘要：推理模型的后训练通常将监督微调与来自可验证奖励的强化学习相结合，最常见的是GRPO。然而，该算法遭受稀疏奖励，有限的探索，和模式崩溃。在最近关于自我蒸馏的工作的基础上，我们提出了反馈蒸馏，这是一种训练方法，其中模型经过训练，以在代币级别匹配其自身的分布，该分布取决于语言模型产生的特权反馈。反馈蒸馏提供代币级监督并可以注入外部知识。在评估我们的Lean 4定理证明方法时，我们发现反馈蒸馏比GRPO在生成轨迹中保持了更大的多样性，从而产生更高的政策熵和更好的传递@k缩放。这两种方法是互补的：从反馈蒸馏检查点初始化GRPO的性能优于单独的任何一种方法。总而言之，我们的结果表明了一种改善复杂推理后训练的有希望的途径。

2605.31494.title: Consolidating Rewarded Perturbations for LLM Post-Training
2605.31494.abstract: arXiv:2605.31494v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Post-training of language models is commonly framed as a sample-score-update loop implemented by gradient descent. A recent line of work, exemplified by RandOpt, relocates this loop to weight space, sampling Gaussian perturbations around a pretrained model and ensembling the top-K rewarded specialists at inference. While competitive with PPO and GRPO under matched training compute, this prediction-level ensemble incurs K forward passes per test example and does not extend cleanly to free-form generation. We ask whether the rewarded population can instead be folded into a single deployable model, replacing the inference-time ensemble with one consolidated update. A split-half analysis over 25 model-task pairs reveals reproducible low-rank structure in every case. We turn this geometry into CoRP (Consolidating Rewarded Perturbations), a gradient-free operator that combines reward-weighted aggregation, compatibility-aware reweighting, and a held-out validation gate, with no gradient flowing through the language model. Across five language models from 0.5B to 8B and five tasks covering math, code, and creative writing, CoRP improves the base model by 8.1 points on average. Using one tenth of RandOpt's perturbation budget, CoRP exceeds single-inference RandOpt by 6.5 points and recovers more than half of the gain of the 50-pass majority-vote ensemble, at one forward pass per test example.
2605.31494.COMMENT: Proposes CoRP, a gradient-free operator that consolidates rewarded perturbations from weight-space RL into a single model, improving LLM post-training without inference-time ensembles.
2605.31494.title_cn: 巩固LLM后培训的奖励扰动
2605.31494.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31494v1宣布类型：新摘要：语言模型的后训练通常被构建为通过梯度下降实现的样本分数更新循环。最近的一项工作以RandOpt为例，将这个循环重新定位到权重空间，对预先训练的模型周围的高斯扰动进行采样，并在推理时汇集前K名获得奖励的专家。虽然在匹配的训练计算下与PPO和GRPO竞争，但这种预测级集成在每个测试示例中会产生K个向前传递，并且不会干净地扩展到自由形式生成。我们问是否可以将奖励人口折叠成一个单一的可部署模型，用一个合并的更新取代推理时间集合。对25个模型-任务对的分半分析表明，在每种情况下都存在可重复的低秩结构。我们将这个几何体转化为CoRP（Consolidating Rewarded Perturbations），这是一个无梯度的运算符，它结合了奖励加权聚合、兼容性感知的重新加权和一个保持的验证门，没有梯度流过语言模型。在0.5B至8B的五种语言模型以及涵盖数学、代码和创意写作的五项任务中，CoRP将基本模型平均提高了8.1个百分点。CoRP使用RandOpt干扰预算的十分之一，比单推理RandOpt高出6.5分，并在每个测试示例向前通过一次时恢复了50次多数票组合一半以上的收益。

2605.30824.title: Planner-Centric Reinforcement Learning for Deep Research with Structure-Aware Reward
2605.30824.abstract: arXiv:2605.30824v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Deep research tasks require LLMs to plan what to investigate, retrieve evidence, and synthesize long-form answers across multiple branches of inquiry. Existing training paradigms either rely on short-form verifiable QA as a proxy or optimize monolithic long trajectories, which makes planning and execution difficult to disentangle and yields weak credit assignment for the planning process. We propose DecomposeR, a planner-centric deep research framework that represents research plans as typed directed acyclic graphs (DAGs), allowing planning to be made explicit, structured, and rewardable. We train a Qwen3-8B model in two stages: planner reinforcement learning (RL) first learns graph structure and query decomposition to improve research planning, and answerer reinforcement learning (RL) then learns branch-level execution and final synthesis conditioned on the learned plan. By assigning rewards to explicit planner tokens and structured components rather than to a flat trajectory, DecomposeR enables finer-grained optimization of planning while reducing the ambiguity of end-to-end training. Experiments show that DecomposeR-8B improves over strong comparable open baselines by 5.1-8.0 points on popular long-form benchmarks due to improved planning and answering capabilities.
2605.30824.COMMENT: Proposes a planner-centric RL framework for deep research with structure-aware rewards on DAGs, enabling fine-grained credit assignment for planning.
2605.30824.title_cn: 以规划者为中心的强化学习用于深度研究，并具有结构感知奖励
2605.30824.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30824v1宣布类型：新摘要：深入的研究任务要求LLM计划调查内容、检索证据并综合跨多个调查分支的长篇答案。现有的培训范式要么依赖于简短的可验证QA作为代理，要么优化单一的长轨迹，这使得规划和执行难以理清，并导致规划过程的信用分配较弱。我们提出了DecomposeR，一个以规划者为中心的深度研究框架，将研究计划表示为类型化的有向无环图（DAG），允许规划变得明确，结构化和可奖励。我们分两个阶段训练Qwen 3 -8B模型：规划者强化学习（RL）首先学习图结构和查询分解以改进研究规划，应答者强化学习（RL）然后学习分支级执行和最终合成。通过将奖励分配给显式规划器令牌和结构化组件，而不是平坦的轨迹，DecomposeR可以实现更细粒度的规划优化，同时减少端到端训练的模糊性。实验表明，由于改进的规划和回答能力，DecomposeR-8B在流行的长形式基准测试中比强可比开放基线提高了5.1-8.0点。

2605.30753.title: Efficient Diffusion LLMs via Temporal-Spatial Parallel Decoding and Confidence Extrapolation
2605.30753.abstract: arXiv:2605.30753v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Diffusion-based large language models (dLLMs) support parallel text generation via iterative denoising, yet inference remains latency-heavy because many steps are spent on redundant refinement and repeated remasking of tokens whose final values are already determined. Prior acceleration methods mainly depend on step-local confidence heuristics or fixed schedules, which are sensitive to prompt and task variation and ignore strong positional effects within a sequence. We cast diffusion decoding as a dynamic control problem and show that token-wise denoising trajectories provide the key signal for reliable control. We propose a trace-aware decoding framework with two components. First, Temporal-Spatial Parallel Decoding (TSPD) uses a lightweight temporalspatial controller that consumes per-token trajectory features, including confidence, entropy, and momentum, together with token position, to decide when a token has converged and can be safely fixed. Second, we introduce Confidence Extrapolation (CE), a training-free state-space module that forecasts future logit trends with uncertainty to support proactive decisions, including safe look-ahead and targeted stabilization when trajectories are oscillatory or underconfident. Together, TSPD and CE reduce unnecessary denoising iterations while preserving output quality, and they compose cleanly with system optimizations such as KV caching.
2605.30753.COMMENT: Proposes a trace-aware decoding framework with temporal-spatial parallel decoding and confidence extrapolation to reduce latency in diffusion LLMs, directly addressing latent reasoning efficiency.
2605.30753.title_cn: 通过时空并行解码和置信度外推的高效扩散LLM
2605.30753.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30753v1宣布类型：新摘要：基于扩散的大型语言模型（dLLM）支持通过迭代去噪来并行文本生成，但推理仍然具有很大的延迟，因为许多步骤都花在对最终值已经确定的标记进行冗余细化和重复重新掩蔽上。现有的加速方法主要依赖于步骤局部置信试探法或固定时间表，这些方法对提示和任务变化敏感，并忽略序列内的强位置效应。我们将扩散解码视为一个动态控制问题，并表明符号式去噪轨迹为可靠控制提供了关键信号。我们提出了一个包含两个部分的踪迹感知解码框架。首先，时空并行解码（TSPD）使用轻量级的时空控制器，该控制器使用每个令牌的轨迹特征，包括置信度、熵和动量，以及令牌位置，来决定令牌何时收敛并可以安全地修复。其次，我们引入了信心外推（CE），这是一个免训练的状态空间模块，可以预测具有不确定性的未来logit趋势，以支持主动决策，包括在轨迹波动或自信不足时的安全前瞻和有针对性的稳定。TSPD和CE共同减少了不必要的去噪迭代，同时保持输出质量，并且与系统优化（例如KV缓存）完美结合。

2605.30833.title: Your Teacher Can't Help You Here: Combating Supervision Fidelity Decay in On-Policy Distillation
2605.30833.abstract: arXiv:2605.30833v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: On-policy distillation transfers reasoning capabilities by training a student model on its own generated trajectories using token-level feedback from a teacher. However, we identify a critical bottleneck, \textbf{Supervision Fidelity Decay (SFD)}: as student-generated prefixes lengthen, the teacher's next-token distribution becomes less confident and less discriminative. Consequently, the teacher-dependent corrective signal in reverse-KL distillation weakens, causing student drift to compound across long reasoning chains. To mitigate SFD, we introduce \textbf{Lookahead Group Reward (\ours{})}. Building on the insight that next-step teacher confidence reflects the discriminative strength of future reverse-KL supervision, \ours{} evaluates the student's top-K candidate tokens by the teacher confidence they induce at the subsequent step and assigns a group-normalized reward. To maintain computational efficiency, we further design an entropy-triggered tree-attention mechanism. Across six math and code benchmarks, \ours{} improves mean@8 by \textbf{2.57} points over OPD for a 7B student, with gains increasing in longer-generation and reaching +\textbf{4.92} points on AIME-26 at 39k tokens.
2605.30833.COMMENT: Proposes Lookahead Group Reward to mitigate supervision fidelity decay in on-policy distillation for LLM reasoning, a novel RL-like reward design for improving student model reasoning.
2605.30833.title_cn: 你的老师无法在这里帮助你：对抗政策上蒸馏中的监督忠实度衰退
2605.30833.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.30833v1宣布类型：新摘要：政策上的提炼通过使用教师的代币级反馈根据其自己生成的轨迹训练学生模型来转移推理能力。然而，我们发现了一个关键的瓶颈，\textBF{Supervision Fidelity Decay（SFD）}：随着学生生成的开头变长，教师的下一个令牌分布变得不那么自信和歧视性。因此，反向KL蒸馏中依赖于教师的纠正信号减弱，导致学生在长推理链中漂移复合。为了减轻SFD，我们引入了\textBF{Lookahead Group Reward（\ours{}）}。基于下一步教师信心反映未来反向KL监督的辨别力这一认识，\our {}通过学生在后续步骤中引发的教师信心来评估学生的前K候选代币，并分配群体标准化奖励。为了保持计算效率，我们进一步设计了一种由信息量触发的树注意机制。在六个数学和代码基准中，对于7 B学生，\our {}比OPD提高了mean@8\textBF{2.57}点，在较长一代中的收益增加，并在AIME-26上达到+\textBF{4.92}点39 k个代币。

2605.31328.title: Reinforcement Learning Amplifies Emergent Misalignment from Harmless Rewards
2605.31328.abstract: arXiv:2605.31328v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Emergent misalignment (EM) is the surprising tendency of language models to become broadly misaligned after fine-tuning on narrowly misaligned examples. While EM has been extensively studied in the supervised fine-tuning (SFT) setting, evidence that it also arises from reinforcement learning (RL) is limited to large, closed-source models, leaving the phenomenon expensive to study and difficult to reproduce. We characterize EM from RL in small, off-the-shelf open-weight models along three axes. First, we show that rewarding narrow, overtly misaligned behavior produces substantially higher general-domain misalignment than sample-matched SFT. Second, we show that EM from RL can be induced by reward signals that could plausibly arise naturally, such as unpopular aesthetic preferences or poor rhetorical appeals. Third, we evaluate in-training mitigations developed for SFT-induced EM and find that they broadly transfer, with interleaving on-policy safety data performing best.
2605.31328.COMMENT: Shows that RL amplifies emergent misalignment from harmless rewards in small open-weight models, studying a key RL safety phenomenon.
2605.31328.title_cn: 强化学习放大了无害奖励中出现的失调
2605.31328.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.31328v1宣布类型：新摘要：紧急失准（EM）是语言模型在对微小失准的示例进行微调后变得广泛失准的令人惊讶的倾向。虽然EM在监督微调（SFT）环境中得到了广泛研究，但它也源于强化学习（RL）的证据仅限于大型闭源模型，导致这种现象的研究成本高昂且难以重现。我们沿着三个轴在小型、现成的开重模型中描述了RL中的EM。首先，我们表明，奖励狭窄、明显失准行为会比样本匹配的SFT产生更高的一般域失准。其次，我们表明RL的EM可以由可能自然产生的奖励信号诱导，例如不受欢迎的审美偏好或糟糕的修辞吸引力。第三，我们评估了为SFT引起的EM开发的训练中缓解措施，并发现它们可以广泛转移，其中交叉的政策安全数据表现最好。
